codeStandardName: OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens Standard
chineseTitle: OSHA 血源性病原体标准
englishTitle: OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens Standard
agency: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), U.S. Department of Labor
coveredEntities: Employers with employees reasonably anticipated to have occupational exposure to blood or other potentially infectious materials, including designated first-aid responders / 预期员工在工作中可能接触血液或其他潜在传染性物质的雇主，包括指定急救人员
keyRequirements: Employers with employees who may be exposed to blood/OPIM must maintain a written Exposure Control Plan, offer the Hepatitis B vaccine, provide PPE, and supply annual training and post-exposure follow-up.
chineseSummary: 该标准要求雇主保护可能在工作中接触血液或其他潜在传染性物质的员工，措施包括书面暴露控制计划、免费乙肝疫苗、个人防护装备、标签警示及暴露后跟踪。1992年3月6日生效，2001年因《针刺安全与预防法》修订加入了工程控制（安全针具）要求。
englishSummary: The standard protects employees who can reasonably be anticipated to contact blood or other potentially infectious materials, requiring a written Exposure Control Plan, free Hepatitis B vaccination, PPE, hazard labeling, training, and post-exposure follow-up. Effective March 6, 1992; amended in 2001 under the Needlestick Safety and Prevention Act to require engineering controls. It applies to a café through designated first-aid responders and clean-up of blood from on-site injuries.
businessImpact: A café whose staff are designated to render first aid or clean up blood from customer/employee injuries triggers the written plan, free Hepatitis B vaccine offer, and training obligations.
inspectionCitationRecord: 1910.1030 is consistently among OSHA's most-cited general-industry health standards; citations frequently issued for missing/outdated Exposure Control Plans and untrained designated first-aiders.
penalty: Up to $16,550 per serious violation; up to $165,514 per willful/repeat violation (2026 OSHA maximums).
recommendedAction: Determine whether any employees are designated first-aiders/clean-up staff; if so, adopt a written Exposure Control Plan, offer the Hepatitis B vaccine, and train annually.

codeStandardName: OSHA Sanitation Standard (General Industry)
chineseTitle: OSHA 卫生标准（一般行业）
englishTitle: OSHA Sanitation Standard (General Industry)
agency: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), U.S. Department of Labor
coveredEntities: All general-industry employers, including retail food-service workplaces such as coffee shops / 所有一般行业雇主，包括咖啡店等零售餐饮工作场所
keyRequirements: Employers must provide clean toilet facilities (separated by sex per Table J-1), potable drinking water, washing facilities with hand soap and drying means, and sanitary waste/food handling — and must allow employees prompt access to restrooms.
chineseSummary: 该标准要求雇主提供符合表 J-1 数量、按性别分隔的清洁厕所、饮用水、配有肥皂和干手设施的洗手设备，并保持垃圾和食品处理的卫生。OSHA 在解释信中明确雇主不得对员工如厕施加不合理限制，必须保证及时使用。源于1971年 OSHA 成立时采纳的标准，是咖啡店员工设施的基础要求。
englishSummary: This foundational standard requires employers to provide clean, sex-separated toilet facilities (per Table J-1), potable water, washing facilities with soap and a drying means, and sanitary handling of waste and food. OSHA interpretations make clear employees must have prompt access to restrooms without unreasonable restrictions. It dates to OSHA's original 1971 standards and governs employee (not customer) facilities in a café.
businessImpact: A café must maintain employee restrooms, potable water, and stocked handwashing stations, and cannot unreasonably restrict staff bathroom breaks — a common inspection trigger.
inspectionCitationRecord: OSHA has repeatedly cited employers under 1910.141(c) for inadequate or restricted access to toilet/washing facilities; numerous standard interpretation letters (e.g., 1998-04-06, 2006-02-02) address prompt-access enforcement.
penalty: Up to $16,550 per serious or other-than-serious violation (2026 OSHA maximums).
recommendedAction: Audit each store for compliant employee restrooms, potable water, and stocked handwashing supplies, and confirm break policies allow prompt restroom access.

codeStandardName: OSHA Respiratory Protection Standard
chineseTitle: OSHA 呼吸防护标准
englishTitle: OSHA Respiratory Protection Standard
agency: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), U.S. Department of Labor
coveredEntities: Employers that require employees to wear respirators, or that permit voluntary respirator use, in general industry / 要求员工佩戴呼吸器、或允许员工自愿使用呼吸器的一般行业雇主
keyRequirements: Where respirators are required, the employer must establish a written respiratory-protection program with medical evaluation, fit testing, and training; even voluntary respirator use triggers Appendix D information and limited program elements.
chineseSummary: 该标准规定，凡要求员工使用呼吸器的雇主必须建立书面呼吸防护计划，包括医学评估、适配性测试和培训。即使是员工自愿佩戴（如自带 N95 口罩），雇主仍须提供附录 D 信息并确保佩戴不会造成危害。1998年4月8日生效，取代1971年旧标准。对咖啡店而言，主要在清洁化学品、烘焙粉尘或员工自愿戴口罩时相关。
englishSummary: Where employers require respirator use, they must implement a written program covering medical evaluation, fit testing, and training; voluntary use (e.g., an employee choosing to wear an N95) triggers Appendix D information and basic provisions. The revised standard took effect April 8, 1998, replacing the 1971 rule. For a café it is most relevant to cleaning chemicals, roasting/grinding dust, or staff voluntarily wearing masks.
businessImpact: If a café ever requires masks/respirators for chemical handling or dust, or formally permits voluntary use, it must provide Appendix D information and potentially medical evaluations and fit testing.
inspectionCitationRecord: 29 CFR 1910.134 is routinely in OSHA's annual Top 10 most-cited standards, frequently for missing written programs, medical evaluations, and fit testing.
penalty: Up to $16,550 per serious violation; up to $165,514 per willful/repeat violation (2026 OSHA maximums).
recommendedAction: Determine whether any task requires respirators; if not, document that respirators are not required and provide Appendix D to any employee who voluntarily wears one.

codeStandardName: OSHA Storage and Handling of Liquefied Petroleum Gases
chineseTitle: OSHA 液化石油气（丙烷）储存与处理标准
englishTitle: OSHA Storage and Handling of Liquefied Petroleum Gases (LPG/Propane)
agency: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), U.S. Department of Labor
coveredEntities: General-industry employers that store, handle, or use liquefied petroleum gas (propane) cylinders / 储存、处理或使用液化石油气（丙烷）钢瓶的一般行业雇主
keyRequirements: Employers must store and handle LPG/propane containers per the standard's design, location, and clearance rules — including locating cylinders to minimize heat exposure, physical damage, and tampering, and not blocking exits or stairways.
chineseSummary: 该标准规定液化石油气（丙烷）钢瓶的设计、储存位置和安全距离要求，包括必须放置在能尽量减少高温、物理损坏和未经授权篡改的位置，室内储存时不得靠近出口、楼梯或通道。源自 OSHA 1971年标准。对使用丙烷供货/室外加热器或备用炊具的咖啡店具有相关性。
englishSummary: 29 CFR 1910.110 sets design, location, and clearance requirements for liquefied petroleum gas (propane) containers, including locating cylinders to minimize temperature rise, physical damage, and tampering, and prohibiting indoor storage near exits, stairways, or access points. It derives from OSHA's 1971 standards. The rule applies to a café that uses propane for backup cooking equipment, patio heaters, or delivery cylinders.
businessImpact: Cafés that keep propane cylinders for patio heaters or backup cooking must store them away from exits and heat sources or face citations and fire-marshal conflicts.
inspectionCitationRecord: OSHA standard interpretations (e.g., 2008-05-23) and citations address improper indoor cylinder storage near exits and heat sources under 1910.110.
penalty: Up to $16,550 per serious violation; up to $165,514 per willful/repeat violation (2026 OSHA maximums).
recommendedAction: Inventory any propane cylinders on site and confirm storage locations comply with clearance, heat, and exit-access requirements; relocate any non-compliant cylinders.

codeStandardName: Americans with Disabilities Act, Title I — Employment
chineseTitle: 《美国残疾人法》第一编——就业
englishTitle: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), Title I — Employment
agency: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC)
coveredEntities: Private employers with 15 or more employees (also state/local governments, employment agencies, labor unions) / 拥有15名或以上员工的私营雇主（以及州/地方政府、职业介绍所、工会）
keyRequirements: Employers with 15+ employees may not discriminate against qualified individuals with disabilities and must provide reasonable accommodation absent undue hardship throughout the employment relationship.
chineseSummary: ADA 第一编禁止拥有15名或以上员工的雇主在招聘、解雇、晋升、薪酬、培训等环节歧视合格的残疾人，并要求在不造成过度负担的前提下提供合理便利。该法1990年通过，1992年7月26日起对25人以上雇主生效，1994年7月26日扩大至15人以上雇主。由 EEOC 执法（29 CFR 1630）。
englishSummary: ADA Title I prohibits employers with 15 or more employees from discriminating against qualified individuals with disabilities and requires reasonable accommodation unless it imposes undue hardship. Enacted in 1990, it first applied to employers with 25+ employees on July 26, 1992, then expanded to employers with 15+ employees on July 26, 1994. The EEOC enforces it through 29 CFR Part 1630.
businessImpact: Once a café (or the chain as a single employer) has 15+ employees, every hiring decision, accommodation request, and termination must comply or risk EEOC charges and litigation.
inspectionCitationRecord: EEOC routinely litigates ADA Title I against restaurant/retail chains; e.g., the agency files and settles numerous disability-discrimination and failure-to-accommodate suits each year against food-service employers.
penalty: Compensatory and punitive damages capped by employer size ($50,000–$300,000), plus back pay, reinstatement, and attorneys' fees under 42 U.S.C. 1981a.
recommendedAction: Adopt a written reasonable-accommodation procedure and train managers on the interactive process and disability non-discrimination once headcount reaches 15.

codeStandardName: ADA Title III — 2010 Standards for Accessible Design & Barrier Removal
chineseTitle: ADA 第三编——2010年无障碍设计标准及障碍清除
englishTitle: ADA Title III — 2010 Standards for Accessible Design and Barrier Removal in Existing Facilities
agency: U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ), Civil Rights Division
codeCitation: 28 CFR Part 36 (incl. 36.304 barrier removal; 36.406 & Appendix re: 2010 ADA Standards)
coveredEntities: Public accommodations, including restaurants and coffee shops, in new construction, alterations, and existing facilities / 公共设施，包括餐厅和咖啡店的新建、改建及现有设施
keyRequirements: New construction and alterations on/after March 15, 2012 must meet the 2010 ADA Standards (accessible entrances, routes, restrooms, counters, parking), and existing facilities must remove architectural barriers where 'readily achievable.'
chineseSummary: 司法部 2010年无障碍设计标准自2012年3月15日起成为强制标准，适用于公共设施的新建和改建，规定了无障碍入口、通道、卫生间、服务台和停车位等技术要求。对于现有设施，28 CFR 36.304 要求在‘易于实现’（readily achievable）的范围内清除建筑障碍。‘安全港’条款保护2012年3月15日前按1991年标准建造的合规要素。咖啡店作为公共设施全面适用。
englishSummary: DOJ's 2010 ADA Standards became the mandatory technical standard on March 15, 2012 for new construction and alterations, governing accessible entrances, routes, restrooms, service counters, and parking. For existing facilities, 28 CFR 36.304 requires removal of architectural barriers where 'readily achievable.' A safe-harbor provision (28 CFR 36.403/36.406) protects elements built to the 1991 Standards before March 15, 2012. As a public accommodation, every café location is covered.
businessImpact: Each new or remodeled café must meet the 2010 design specs (e.g., a portion of counter ≤36 in. high, accessible routes/restrooms), and existing stores face serial ADA access lawsuits if readily achievable barriers remain.
inspectionCitationRecord: Restaurants and cafés are frequent targets of private ADA Title III 'drive-by' lawsuits and DOJ settlements over inaccessible entrances, counters, and restrooms; thousands of Title III suits are filed annually.
penalty: DOJ civil penalties up to $104,861 (first violation) / $209,722 (subsequent) plus injunctive relief; private suits recover injunctive relief and attorneys' fees (and state-law damages).
recommendedAction: Commission an ADA access survey of each location against the 2010 Standards and remediate readily-achievable barriers; design all new builds/remodels to the 2010 Standards.

codeStandardName: DOJ ADA Title II Rule on Accessibility of Web Content and Mobile Apps
chineseTitle: 司法部 ADA 第二编网站与移动应用无障碍最终规则
englishTitle: DOJ Final Rule: Accessibility of Web Content and Mobile Apps Provided by State and Local Governments (ADA Title II)
agency: U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ), Civil Rights Division
codeCitation: 28 CFR Part 35 (Title II); final rule published 89 FR 31320 (Apr. 24, 2024)
coveredEntities: State and local governments (Title II public entities); directly binding on public entities, not private cafés / 州和地方政府（第二编公共实体）；直接约束公共实体，而非私营咖啡店
keyRequirements: State and local government web content and mobile apps must conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA, with compliance phased by entity population size.
chineseSummary: 司法部于2024年4月24日发布 ADA 第二编最终规则（规则本身于2024年6月24日生效），要求州和地方政府的网站和移动应用符合 WCAG 2.1 AA 级技术标准。合规期限按人口分阶段：大型实体（人口≥5万）和小型实体/特别区原定2026年和2027年到期；司法部随后于2026年4月发布临时最终规则将期限各延长一年（大型实体至2027年4月26日，小型实体/特别区至2028年4月26日）。该规则直接约束公共实体，对咖啡店属信息参考——预示数字无障碍执法趋势，并影响政府场所内特许经营点的要求。
englishSummary: DOJ's April 24, 2024 final rule (effective June 24, 2024) requires state and local governments' websites and mobile apps to meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA, phased by population. Original compliance dates (large entities by April 24, 2026; smaller entities/special districts by April 26, 2027) were each extended one year by an April 2026 interim final rule, to April 26, 2027 and April 26, 2028 respectively. It binds public (Title II) entities directly, not private cafés. It is informational for Luckin as a signal of intensifying digital-accessibility enforcement and a potential requirement for kiosks/apps in government-operated venues.
businessImpact: Not directly binding on a private café, but it foreshadows digital-accessibility expectations (app, online ordering, kiosks) and conditions for operating concessions inside government-owned locations.
penalty: Not directly applicable to private accommodations; Title II is enforced via DOJ action and private suits for injunctive relief against public entities.
recommendedAction: Treat WCAG 2.1 AA as the de-facto benchmark for the Luckin app/website to pre-empt Title III web-accessibility suits and government-venue concession requirements.

codeStandardName: EEOC Final Rule Implementing the Pregnant Workers Fairness Act
chineseTitle: EEOC 实施《孕期工作者公平法》最终规则
englishTitle: EEOC Final Rule Implementing the Pregnant Workers Fairness Act (PWFA)
agency: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC)
coveredEntities: Employers with 15 or more employees (private and public) / 拥有15名或以上员工的私营和公共部门雇主
keyRequirements: Covered employers must provide reasonable accommodations for known limitations related to pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions (e.g., seating, extra breaks, schedule changes) unless it causes undue hardship.
chineseSummary: 《孕期工作者公平法》要求拥有15名或以上员工的雇主，为员工或求职者已知的、与怀孕、分娩或相关医疗状况有关的限制提供合理便利（如坐凳、额外休息、轻量化工作、调整排班），除非造成过度负担。该法本身于2023年6月27日生效，EEOC 最终实施规则于2024年6月18日生效。咖啡店常见的便利包括允许怀孕咖啡师坐着、更频繁如厕和喝水休息。
englishSummary: The PWFA requires employers with 15+ employees to provide reasonable accommodations for known limitations arising from pregnancy, childbirth, or related conditions — such as seating, more frequent breaks, light duty, or schedule changes — unless it imposes undue hardship. The statute took effect June 27, 2023, and the EEOC's final implementing regulation became effective June 18, 2024. Typical café accommodations include letting a pregnant barista sit, take water/restroom breaks, or avoid heavy lifting.
businessImpact: A café with 15+ employees must grant reasonable accommodations to pregnant baristas (seating, breaks, lifting limits) and cannot force them onto leave if an accommodation would let them keep working.
inspectionCitationRecord: EEOC began accepting PWFA charges June 27, 2023 and has filed enforcement litigation against employers for denying pregnancy accommodations and forcing leave.
penalty: Same remedies as Title VII: compensatory/punitive damages capped $50,000–$300,000 by size, plus back pay and attorneys' fees.
recommendedAction: Update accommodation policies and train store managers to engage in the interactive process for pregnancy-related requests rather than defaulting to leave.

codeStandardName: OSHA Improve Tracking of Workplace Injuries and Illnesses (Electronic Submission Expansion)
chineseTitle: OSHA 改进工伤与职业病追踪规则（电子报送扩展）
englishTitle: OSHA Final Rule: Improve Tracking of Workplace Injuries and Illnesses (Electronic Submission)
agency: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), U.S. Department of Labor
coveredEntities: Establishments with 20-249 employees in designated industries (Form 300A) and 100+ employees in high-hazard industries (Forms 300/301) / 指定行业中20-249名员工的场所（300A表）及高危行业中100名以上员工的场所（300/301表）
keyRequirements: Covered establishments must electronically submit injury/illness summary data (Form 300A) annually by March 2, and high-hazard establishments with 100+ employees must also submit Form 300 and 301 details including the establishment's legal company name.
chineseSummary: OSHA 于2023年7月17日发布最终规则，自2024年1月1日生效，扩大了电子报送要求。指定行业中20-249名员工的场所须每年（3月2日前）通过 ITA 系统电子提交 300A 工伤汇总表；列入附录 B 的高危行业中拥有100名以上员工的场所还须提交 300 和 301 表详细信息。报送须包含场所法定公司名称。咖啡店是否适用取决于其 NAICS 行业代码和单店员工人数。
englishSummary: OSHA's final rule (issued July 17, 2023; effective January 1, 2024) expands electronic reporting under 29 CFR 1904.41. Establishments with 20-249 employees in designated industries must electronically submit Form 300A summary data each year by March 2 via the Injury Tracking Application, and establishments with 100+ employees in high-hazard Appendix B industries must also submit Form 300 and 301 detail, including the legal company name. Whether a café is covered depends on its NAICS code and per-establishment headcount.
businessImpact: Larger café locations (or any meeting the size/NAICS thresholds) must keep accurate OSHA injury logs and electronically file them by March 2 each year or face recordkeeping citations.
inspectionCitationRecord: OSHA cites employers under 1904.41 for failure to electronically submit; the agency publicizes injury data and uses non-submission to target enforcement.
penalty: Up to $16,550 per other-than-serious recordkeeping/reporting violation (2026 OSHA maximums).
recommendedAction: Confirm each establishment's NAICS code and headcount against the covered-industry lists, maintain OSHA 300/300A/301 logs, and file electronically by March 2 where required.

codeStandardName: FLSA Child Labor — Hazardous Occupations & Youth Employment in Restaurants
chineseTitle: 《公平劳动标准法》童工——危险职业及餐饮业青少年雇佣规定
englishTitle: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Child Labor Provisions — Hazardous Occupations and Youth Employment in Restaurants
agency: Wage and Hour Division (WHD), U.S. Department of Labor
coveredEntities: Employers of minors under 18 in nonagricultural jobs, including restaurants and quick-service establishments / 在非农岗位雇佣18岁以下未成年人的雇主，包括餐厅和快餐店
keyRequirements: Employers must observe hours/time-of-day limits for 14- and 15-year-olds and the hazardous-occupations bans for workers under 18 (e.g., no operating power-driven dough mixers, slicers, or most baking machines, and limited driving).
chineseSummary: FLSA 童工条款（29 CFR Part 570）规定了餐饮业雇佣未成年人的标准：14-15岁有严格的工时和时段限制；所有18岁以下员工禁止从事危险职业，包括操作动力驱动的搅拌机、面团机、切片机及大多数烘焙机械，并限制驾驶。这些危险职业令（Hazardous Orders）历经多次修订（如2010年更新），是咖啡店雇佣青少年咖啡师/学徒时的核心合规点。
englishSummary: The FLSA child-labor rules at 29 CFR Part 570 set hours and time-of-day limits for 14- and 15-year-olds and ban hazardous occupations for all workers under 18 — including operating power-driven dough mixers, slicers, and most bakery machines, and most driving. The hazardous-occupation orders have been amended repeatedly (e.g., HO updates effective 2010; WHD Fact Sheet #2A last revised July 2010). They are central whenever a café hires teenage baristas or food-prep workers.
businessImpact: A café hiring teen baristas must restrict their hours and bar them from power-driven mixers/slicers and certain equipment, or face per-minor child-labor penalties and reputational damage.
inspectionCitationRecord: WHD has dramatically increased child-labor enforcement, assessing record penalties against food-service and franchise employers (e.g., multimillion-dollar fast-food franchisee settlements for minors working illegal hours/equipment).
penalty: Up to $16,035 per minor per violation; up to $72,876 where a violation causes death or serious injury to a worker under 18 (doublable for willful/repeat).
recommendedAction: Implement age-coded scheduling and an equipment-restriction policy for employees under 18, and verify minors' ages and work permits at hire.

codeStandardName: New York Retail Worker Safety Act (Workplace Violence Prevention for Retail Employees)
chineseTitle: 纽约州《零售从业者安全法》——零售场所工作场所暴力预防
englishTitle: New York Retail Worker Safety Act — Retail Workplace Violence Prevention
agency: New York State Department of Labor (NYSDOL)
codeCitation: N.Y. Labor Law § 27-e (Chapter 308 of 2024, as amended by S.B. 8358-C)
coveredEntities: 在纽约州雇佣10名或以上‘零售员工’的雇主，且‘零售店’不包括主要供堂食的餐饮场所（餐厅、咖啡馆通常被排除在外） / Employers with 10+ retail employees in NY; a 'retail store' excludes businesses primarily selling food for on-premises consumption, so restaurants and most cafés are generally NOT covered
keyRequirements: Covered retail employers must adopt a written workplace violence prevention policy, provide workplace violence prevention training (on hire, then every 2 years for employers with 10–49 employees / annually for 50+), and distribute the policy and a notice to employees. A 'retail store' is one selling consumer commodities directly to the public at retail and not primarily engaged in selling food for on-premises consumption.
chineseSummary: 该法要求在纽约州雇佣10名及以上零售员工的雇主制定书面工作场所暴力预防政策、向员工发放该政策与告示，并提供暴力预防培训。主要条款于2025年6月2日生效；自2027年1月1日起，雇佣500名及以上零售员工的雇主还须配备紧急‘静默报警按钮’。重要：‘零售店’不包括主要供堂食的餐饮场所，因此餐厅及大多数咖啡馆通常不在适用范围内。
englishSummary: Employers with 10+ retail employees in NY must adopt a written workplace-violence-prevention policy, distribute it with a notice, and train staff (every two years for 10–49 employees, annually for 50+). Core obligations took effect June 2, 2025; a panic/silent-response-button provision applies to employers with 500+ retail employees starting January 1, 2027. Importantly, the statutory definition of 'retail store' excludes businesses primarily engaged in selling food for on-premises consumption, so restaurants and most cafés are generally outside the Act's scope.
businessImpact: A coffee chain that operates as food service (selling food/beverages for on-premises consumption) is generally NOT a covered 'retail store' under the Act and likely falls outside its policy/training mandate. The obligation would only attach to a true retail format (e.g., a packaged-goods retail store). Confirm format and headcount before assuming coverage.
inspectionCitationRecord: NYSDOL published a Model Retail Workplace Violence Prevention Policy and model training materials (2025); enforcement is via NYSDOL with the Attorney General able to seek injunctive relief and penalties for non-compliance.
penalty: Subject to NYSDOL/Attorney General enforcement; injunctive relief and civil penalties for failure to maintain policy/training (no fixed per-violation schedule published).
recommendedAction: Confirm whether any NY location is a 'retail store' (not primarily food-for-on-premises-consumption) and counts 10+ retail employees; only then adopt NYSDOL's model policy and document training. Food-service cafés are generally exempt but should monitor any DOL guidance changes.

codeStandardName: 2025 Uniform Fire Prevention and Building Code of New York State (19 NYCRR Parts 1220–1227)
chineseTitle: 2025年《纽约州统一防火与建筑规范》（19 NYCRR 第1220–1227部分）
englishTitle: 2025 New York State Uniform Fire Prevention and Building Code
agency: NYS Department of State, Division of Building Standards and Codes (Fire Prevention and Building Code Council)
codeCitation: 19 NYCRR Parts 1220–1227 (2025 editions based on 2024 ICC model codes)
coveredEntities: 在纽约州（纽约市以外）进行新建、改建或装修并申请建筑许可的所有受监管方，包括咖啡店选址装修 / All parties filing building-permit applications for construction or alteration in NY outside NYC, including coffee-shop fit-outs
keyRequirements: Building, mechanical, plumbing, fire, fuel-gas, property-maintenance and existing-building work permitted on or after the effective date must comply with the 2025 Uniform Code (2024 ICC-based) editions.
chineseSummary: 纽约州采用基于2024版ICC示范规范的2025版统一规范，取代2020版。该规范于2025年12月31日生效，并设有2025年10月1日至12月30日的过渡期，期间申请人可选择适用2020或2025版；过渡期后无宽限期。咖啡店新店装修的建筑、机械、给排水和消防工程均须符合现行版本。
englishSummary: NYS adopted the 2025 Uniform Code (based on the 2024 ICC model codes), replacing the 2020 edition. It took effect December 31, 2025, with a transition window (Oct 1–Dec 30, 2025) allowing either the 2020 or 2025 code, and no grace period afterward. Coffee-shop build-outs outside NYC must meet the current edition for building, mechanical, plumbing, and fire work.
businessImpact: Any new or remodeled café location outside NYC permitted after Dec 31, 2025 must be designed and built to the 2025 code, affecting exits, occupancy, accessibility, mechanical exhaust, and plumbing in the buildout.
inspectionCitationRecord: Enforced by local code enforcement officials via permit review, inspections, and certificates of occupancy/compliance under 19 NYCRR Part 1203; non-compliant work can be denied a CO.
penalty: Local enforcement; stop-work orders, denial of certificate of occupancy/compliance, and municipal fines (vary by locality, commonly up to $1,000/day per violation under local ordinances).
recommendedAction: Confirm that any new-store design and permit application outside NYC is prepared to the 2025 Uniform Code (2024 ICC-based) and coordinate the edition with the local code-enforcement officer before filing.

codeStandardName: 2020 Energy Conservation Construction Code of New York State (NYSECCC)
chineseTitle: 2020年《纽约州节能建筑施工规范》（NYSECCC）
englishTitle: 2020 Energy Conservation Construction Code of New York State
agency: NYS Department of State, Division of Building Standards and Codes
codeCitation: 19 NYCRR Part 1240 (2020 ECCCNYS; based on 2018 IECC and ASHRAE 90.1-2016)
coveredEntities: 在纽约州（纽约市以外）进行新建及改建商业建筑的受监管方，包括咖啡店租赁空间装修 / Parties constructing or altering commercial buildings in NY outside NYC, including leased café fit-outs
keyRequirements: Permitted commercial construction and alterations must meet the energy code's requirements for building envelope, HVAC, service water heating, and lighting (2018 IECC commercial provisions / ASHRAE 90.1-2016).
chineseSummary: 2020版NYSECCC于2020年5月12日生效，无过渡期，依据2018 IECC和ASHRAE 90.1-2016制定，对商业建筑围护结构、暖通空调、生活热水和照明提出节能要求。咖啡店在纽约州（市外）的租赁装修须满足这些要求；该版本已于2025年12月31日被2025版能源规范取代用于新许可申请。
englishSummary: The 2020 NYSECCC took effect May 12, 2020 (no transition period) and applies the 2018 IECC commercial provisions and ASHRAE 90.1-2016, governing envelope, HVAC, service water heating, and lighting efficiency. Café tenant fit-outs outside NYC had to comply; for permits filed on/after Dec 31, 2025 the 2025 Energy Code now applies instead.
businessImpact: Lighting, HVAC, and envelope choices in a café buildout drive energy-code compliance documentation that can hold up the permit and certificate of occupancy if the design isn't code-compliant.
inspectionCitationRecord: Enforced via permit/plan review and inspections by local code enforcement; compliance typically demonstrated using COMcheck or prescriptive paths before CO issuance.
penalty: Local enforcement; permit denial, stop-work orders, and municipal fines for non-compliant work.
recommendedAction: Have the design team document energy-code compliance (e.g., COMcheck) for lighting and HVAC at permit filing, using the edition in force at filing date.

codeStandardName: NYC Fire Code 2022 — Commercial Cooking Fire-Extinguishing Systems (FC 904.3.8)
chineseTitle: 2022年《纽约市消防规范》——商业烹饪灭火系统（FC 904.3.8）
englishTitle: 2022 NYC Fire Code — Commercial Cooking Fire-Extinguishing Systems (FC 904.3.8)
agency: Fire Department of the City of New York (FDNY)
codeCitation: NYC Fire Code (Title 29 / FC) § 904.3.8.1–904.3.8.3 (wet chemical per NFPA 17A, UL 300); 2022 Fire Code adopted by Local Law 47 of 2022
coveredEntities: 纽约市内设有商业烹饪设备并产生油脂蒸气的食品企业（如带热厨灶的咖啡店/餐饮） / NYC food establishments with commercial cooking appliances producing grease-laden vapors, including cafés with hot cooking
keyRequirements: Commercial cooking operations producing grease-laden vapors must be protected by an approved, UL 300-listed wet-chemical (or equivalent foam-water/water-mist) fire-extinguishing system; non-UL-300 dry-chemical, non-listed wet-chemical, and CO2 systems are prohibited and must be removed and replaced.
chineseSummary: 纽约市2022年消防规范（经2022年第47号地方法采纳，2022年4月15日生效）要求产生油脂蒸气的商业烹饪作业安装经认可、符合UL 300标准的湿式化学灭火系统（FC 904.3.8.1）。不符合UL 300的干粉、未列名湿式化学及二氧化碳系统被禁止，必须更换。系统须由持FDNY资格证人员定期维护。
englishSummary: Under the 2022 NYC Fire Code (the 2022 Fire Code was adopted via Local Law 47 of 2022, effective April 15, 2022), commercial cooking that produces grease-laden vapors must have an approved UL 300-listed wet-chemical fire-extinguishing system (or equivalent) per FC 904.3.8.1. Dry-chemical, non-listed wet-chemical, and CO2 systems are barred and must be removed and replaced. Systems require periodic FDNY-credentialed servicing.
businessImpact: A café that adds grilling, panini, or other grease-producing cooking must install and maintain a code-compliant UL 300 hood suppression system or face FDNY violations and a shutdown risk.
inspectionCitationRecord: FDNY inspections cite non-UL-300 or unserviced kitchen suppression systems; FDNY notices of violation are returnable to the OATH Hearings Division.
penalty: FDNY violations adjudicated at OATH; civil penalties commonly run into the thousands of dollars per violation, with orders to correct/replace non-compliant systems.
recommendedAction: If any NYC location does grease-producing cooking, verify the hood suppression system is UL 300-listed and on a current FDNY-credentialed service contract.

codeStandardName: FDNY Certificate of Fitness S-17 — Commercial Cooking Wet Chemical Fire Extinguishing Systems
chineseTitle: FDNY S-17 资格证——商业烹饪湿式化学灭火系统
englishTitle: FDNY Certificate of Fitness S-17 — Commercial Cooking Wet Chemical Fire Extinguishing Systems
agency: Fire Department of the City of New York (FDNY)
codeCitation: 3 RCNY (FDNY rules) / NYC Fire Code Ch. 9; servicing per NFPA 17A and FDNY rules
coveredEntities: 在纽约市安装、检查、测试或维护商业烹饪湿式化学灭火系统的人员，以及聘用其服务的餐饮场所 / Persons who install/inspect/test/maintain commercial cooking wet-chemical systems in NYC and the establishments engaging them
keyRequirements: Any individual who installs, inspects, tests, or maintains a commercial cooking wet-chemical fire-extinguishing system in NYC must hold an FDNY S-17 Certificate of Fitness (employed by an S-71 holder's company), and fusible links must be replaced every six months or after any discharge.
chineseSummary: 在纽约市对商业烹饪湿式化学灭火系统进行安装、检查、测试或维护的人员必须持有FDNY S-17资格证，并受雇于S-71持证人的公司。根据NFPA 17A和FDNY规则，系统熔断片须每六个月或每次喷放后更换。S-17资格证有效期三年。咖啡店应确保其消防系统服务商持有有效S-17证。
englishSummary: Servicing of NYC commercial cooking wet-chemical fire-extinguishing systems may only be performed by a holder of the FDNY S-17 Certificate of Fitness, employed by an S-71 certificate holder's company. Per NFPA 17A and FDNY rules, fusible links must be replaced every six months or after a discharge. The S-17 renews every three years.
businessImpact: A café with a hood suppression system must hire a vendor whose technicians hold valid S-17 certificates and keep semiannual service records, or the system is deemed non-compliant on FDNY inspection.
inspectionCitationRecord: FDNY inspections require proof the kitchen suppression system was serviced by an S-17 holder; missing/expired service tags are cited.
penalty: Fines reported up to roughly $5,000 for violations identified during FDNY fire inspections; orders to correct.
recommendedAction: Confirm the kitchen-suppression service vendor's technicians hold current S-17 certificates and retain semiannual service tags/records on site.

codeStandardName: NYC Fire Code — Portable Fire Extinguishers, including Class K for Commercial Cooking (FC 906)
chineseTitle: 纽约市消防规范——便携式灭火器（含商业烹饪K类灭火器）（FC 906）
englishTitle: NYC Fire Code — Portable Fire Extinguishers, incl. Class K (FC 906)
agency: Fire Department of the City of New York (FDNY)
codeCitation: NYC Fire Code § FC 906; maintenance per NFPA 10 and 3 RCNY § 15-02
coveredEntities: 纽约市所有商业场所，特别是设有商业烹饪设备的食品企业 / All NYC commercial premises, especially food establishments with commercial cooking appliances
keyRequirements: Businesses must provide, properly mount, and maintain portable fire extinguishers (with a Class K extinguisher within 30 feet of commercial cooking appliances using combustible cooking media), with monthly visual checks and annual professional inspection/maintenance per NFPA 10.
chineseSummary: 纽约市消防规范FC 906要求商业场所配备、正确安装并维护便携式灭火器；使用可燃烹饪介质的商业烹饪设备30英尺范围内须配置K类灭火器。须每月目视检查、每年由专业人员检验维护（依NFPA 10及3 RCNY §15-02），并保留检验标签。
englishSummary: FC 906 requires NYC businesses to provide, mount, and maintain portable fire extinguishers, including a Class K extinguisher within 30 ft of commercial cooking appliances using combustible cooking media. Extinguishers need monthly quick checks plus annual professional inspection and maintenance per NFPA 10 (and 3 RCNY §15-02), with current service tags retained.
businessImpact: Every café needs correctly placed, tagged, and annually serviced extinguishers — plus a Class K unit if there is any deep-fat/grease cooking — to pass FDNY inspection.
inspectionCitationRecord: FDNY routinely cites missing/expired-tag extinguishers and absent Class K units near cooking lines; violations returnable to OATH.
penalty: FDNY notices of violation adjudicated at OATH; civil penalties typically several hundred to a few thousand dollars per violation.
recommendedAction: Schedule annual extinguisher servicing with an FDNY-approved company, do monthly visual checks, and add a Class K extinguisher near any grease-producing cooking.

codeStandardName: Cross-Connection Control / Backflow Prevention (10 NYCRR Part 5, § 5-1.31)
chineseTitle: 交叉连接控制／防回流（10 NYCRR 第5部分，§5-1.31）
englishTitle: Cross-Connection Control / Backflow Prevention (10 NYCRR § 5-1.31)
agency: New York State Department of Health (and the local public water supplier)
codeCitation: 10 NYCRR Subpart 5-1, § 5-1.31 (Public Health Law Art. 11); devices per NYS Plumbing Code Ch. 6
coveredEntities: 所有连接公共供水系统、存在交叉连接风险的用户，包括带咖啡/餐饮设备（浓缩咖啡机、制冰机、化学清洗）的咖啡店 / All users of public water systems with cross-connection hazards, including cafés with espresso machines, ice makers, and chemical dispensers
keyRequirements: Premises with potential cross-connections must install approved backflow prevention devices, and each device must be tested by a NYS-certified backflow tester at installation, after any repair, and at least annually, with records kept by the water supplier.
chineseSummary: 依据10 NYCRR §5-1.31，凡存在潜在交叉连接危害的供水用户须安装经认可的防回流装置，以保护公共饮用水。装置须在安装时、每次维修后及至少每年由纽约州认证的防回流检测员进行检测，记录由供水方保存。咖啡店的浓缩咖啡机、制冰机、软水/化学投加设备常触发此要求。
englishSummary: Under 10 NYCRR § 5-1.31, water users with potential cross-connection hazards must install approved backflow prevention devices to protect the public water supply. Each device must be tested by a NYS-certified backflow prevention device tester at installation, after each repair, and at least annually, with records maintained by the water supplier. Café equipment such as espresso machines, ice makers, and chemical dosing commonly triggers this requirement.
businessImpact: A café typically must install and file annual backflow test reports for its water connection, and a lapsed test can trigger water-supplier notices or service interruption.
inspectionCitationRecord: Local water suppliers (e.g., NYC DEP for NYC premises) enforce via annual test-report tracking and non-compliance notices; the NYS DOH oversees tester certification under § 5-1.31.
penalty: Enforced by the water supplier/health department; notices of violation, potential discontinuance of water service, and Public Health Law penalties for failure to test.
recommendedAction: Identify required backflow devices for water-using equipment and schedule a NYS-certified tester to file annual test reports with the water supplier.

codeStandardName: ADA Title I Employment Provisions (with New York State Human Rights Law overlay)
chineseTitle: ADA第一章就业条款（叠加纽约州《人权法》）
englishTitle: ADA Title I — Employment (with New York State Human Rights Law)
agency: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC); NYS Division of Human Rights for NYSHRL
codeCitation: 42 U.S.C. §§ 12111–12117; 29 CFR Part 1630; N.Y. Exec. Law Art. 15 (Human Rights Law)
coveredEntities: 雇佣15名及以上员工的雇主（ADA）；纽约州《人权法》适用于纽约州内所有雇主（不论规模） / Employers with 15+ employees (ADA); the NYSHRL applies to all NY employers regardless of size
keyRequirements: Employers must not discriminate against qualified individuals with disabilities and must provide reasonable accommodation absent undue hardship; under the ADA this applies at 15+ employees, while the NYS Human Rights Law imposes parallel disability-accommodation duties on all NY employers.
chineseSummary: ADA第一章禁止对符合资格的残障人士在就业中歧视，并要求在不构成过度负担的情况下提供合理便利；联邦门槛为15名及以上员工。纽约州《人权法》对州内所有规模的雇主施加类似的残障合理便利义务，因此小型咖啡店即使低于联邦门槛也须遵守州法。
englishSummary: ADA Title I bars employment discrimination against qualified individuals with disabilities and requires reasonable accommodation absent undue hardship; the federal duty attaches at 15+ employees. New York's Human Rights Law imposes parallel disability-accommodation obligations on all employers in the state regardless of size, so even a small café below the federal threshold must comply under state law.
businessImpact: A growing coffee chain must engage in the interactive accommodation process for disabled applicants and baristas — and NYS law applies even at single small stores below 15 employees.
inspectionCitationRecord: Enforced by EEOC charges (federal) and NYS Division of Human Rights complaints; failure-to-accommodate and disability-bias claims are common in food-service/retail.
penalty: ADA: compensatory/punitive damages capped by employer size ($50,000–$300,000) plus back pay; NYSHRL: uncapped compensatory damages and civil penalties.
recommendedAction: Adopt a written reasonable-accommodation/interactive-process procedure and train managers, applying it to all stores given the NYSHRL's all-size coverage.

codeStandardName: NYC Local Law 97 of 2019 — Building Greenhouse Gas Emissions Limits
chineseTitle: 纽约市2019年第97号地方法——建筑温室气体排放限值
englishTitle: NYC Local Law 97 of 2019 — Building Greenhouse Gas Emissions Limits
agency: NYC Department of Buildings (Office of Building Energy and Emissions Performance)
codeCitation: NYC Administrative Code § 28-320 (Local Law 97 of 2019); Climate Mobilization Act
coveredEntities: 纽约市内单栋超过25,000平方英尺（或符合条件的多栋）建筑的业主；承租咖啡店通过租约和共用系统受影响 / Owners of NYC buildings over 25,000 sq ft (or qualifying multi-building lots); tenant cafés affected via leases and shared systems
keyRequirements: Covered buildings over 25,000 sq ft must keep annual greenhouse-gas emissions below tightening limits (first compliance period 2024–2029) and file an annual emissions report by May 1, with stricter caps beginning in 2030.
chineseSummary: 2019年第97号地方法要求纽约市超过25,000平方英尺的建筑将年度温室气体排放控制在逐步收紧的限值内，首个达标期为2024–2029年，年度排放报告须在每年5月1日前提交。超限处罚为每吨二氧化碳当量268美元。虽属业主义务，但咖啡店的能耗与租约条款会受影响。
englishSummary: Local Law 97 caps annual greenhouse-gas emissions for NYC buildings over 25,000 sq ft, with the first compliance period (2024–2029) and an annual emissions report due each May 1; caps tighten further from 2030. Penalties run $268 per metric ton of CO2-equivalent over the limit, plus filing penalties. Though an owner obligation, café energy use and lease pass-throughs are directly affected.
businessImpact: A café leasing space in a large NYC building may face emissions-related lease pass-through charges and pressure to electrify equipment and cut energy use to help the landlord avoid LL97 penalties.
inspectionCitationRecord: NYC DOB enforces via annual report filings (first reports due May 1, 2025 for the 2024 reporting year); ~50,000 buildings are covered.
penalty: $268 per metric ton CO2e over the limit annually; failure to file = $0.50/sq ft per month; false reports up to $500,000.
recommendedAction: For NYC leased locations, review lease energy/emissions cost-recovery clauses and prioritize energy-efficient, electrified equipment to limit landlord LL97 exposure passed to tenants.

codeStandardName: NYC Local Law 26 of 2004 — High-Rise Office Building Sprinkler Retrofit
chineseTitle: 纽约市2004年第26号地方法——高层办公楼喷淋系统改造
englishTitle: NYC Local Law 26 of 2004 — High-Rise Office Building Sprinkler Retrofit
agency: NYC Department of Buildings (DOB) / FDNY
codeCitation: Local Law 26 of 2004 (NYC Building Code high-rise sprinkler retrofit provisions); compliance deadline July 1, 2019
coveredEntities: 纽约市高度达到或超过100英尺的办公（商业）建筑业主；承租其中的咖啡店间接受影响 / Owners of NYC office (business) buildings 100 ft or taller; cafés tenanted in such buildings are indirectly affected
keyRequirements: Existing office/business occupancy high-rise buildings of 100 feet or more (in existence on Oct 22, 2004) had to be fully protected by an automatic sprinkler system (plus photoluminescent exit markings) by the July 1, 2019 deadline.
chineseSummary: 2004年第26号地方法（为回应9·11而制定）要求纽约市高度达到或超过100英尺的既有办公楼在2019年7月1日前全面安装自动喷淋系统，并设置发光疏散标识。这是业主义务，但位于此类高层建筑内的咖啡店应核实楼宇喷淋合规状态，因其直接关系到自身的消防安全与疏散条件。
englishSummary: Local Law 26 of 2004 (enacted after 9/11) required existing NYC office high-rises of 100 feet or more (in existence on October 22, 2004) to be fully sprinklered, with photoluminescent exit markings, by the July 1, 2019 deadline. It is an owner obligation, but a café tenanted in such a tower should confirm the building's sprinkler-compliance status because it governs the café's own fire protection and egress conditions.
businessImpact: A café occupying ground-floor or lobby space in an older NYC office tower depends on the landlord's LL26 sprinkler compliance for fire-safety sign-off and may be exposed if the building remains in violation.
inspectionCitationRecord: As of reporting, a number of buildings remained non-compliant, with many issued Environmental Control Board (OATH) violations.
penalty: Reported violations adjudicated through OATH/ECB for continued non-compliance.
recommendedAction: Before signing a lease in a pre-2004 NYC high-rise office building of 100+ ft, confirm the building's Local Law 26 sprinkler compliance status with the landlord and DOB records.

codeStandardName: New Jersey State Uniform Construction Code Act (Uniform Construction Code)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州统一建筑规范法（统一建筑规范）
englishTitle: New Jersey State Uniform Construction Code Act
agency: New Jersey Department of Community Affairs (DCA), Division of Codes and Standards
coveredEntities: All persons constructing, altering, renovating, or changing the use/occupancy of any building in NJ / 在新泽西州进行建造、改建、翻新或变更用途的所有主体
keyRequirements: Establishes a single statewide construction code (no local home-rule codes) requiring construction permits and certificates before building, altering, or occupying any structure.
chineseSummary: 1975年颁布的《统一建筑规范法》（P.L. 1975, c. 217）授权社区事务部制定全州统一的建筑规范，取代地方各自的规范。新泽西所有市镇均按 N.J.A.C. 5:23 执行，任何建造、改建或用途变更均须先取得施工许可证及入住证。
englishSummary: The 1975 State Uniform Construction Code Act (P.L. 1975, c. 217) authorized DCA to adopt one statewide construction code, replacing locally-adopted codes. Every NJ municipality enforces N.J.A.C. 5:23, and any construction, alteration, or change of use requires a construction permit and certificate before occupancy.
businessImpact: Every new Luckin Coffee buildout, tenant fit-out, or equipment alteration in NJ must obtain DCA-system construction permits and pass subcode inspections before opening.
penalty: Civil penalties up to $2,000 per violation under N.J.S.A. 52:27D-138; stop-work orders for unpermitted work.
recommendedAction: Engage a NJ-licensed architect/engineer to file permits with the local construction office for every store buildout and major equipment change.

codeStandardName: New Jersey Building Subcode (2021 International Building Code, NJ Edition)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州建筑分规范（2021年国际建筑规范，新泽西版）
englishTitle: New Jersey Building Subcode (2021 IBC New Jersey Edition)
agency: New Jersey Department of Community Affairs (DCA), Division of Codes and Standards
coveredEntities: Owners/designers of new and altered commercial buildings, including mercantile (Group M) and assembly (Group A-2) occupancies / 新建及改建商业建筑（含商业用途M类、聚集用途A-2类）的业主与设计方
keyRequirements: Adopts the 2021 International Building Code (with NJ amendments) as the building subcode governing structural, occupancy, egress, and fire-resistance design for commercial fit-outs.
chineseSummary: 新泽西在 N.J.A.C. 5:23-3.14 采用经修订的2021年国际建筑规范作为建筑分规范，2022年9月6日生效（含约6个月的过渡宽限期）。该分规范规定商铺的占用分类、安全出口、防火分隔及结构要求。
englishSummary: NJ adopts the 2021 IBC (with NJ amendments) as its Building Subcode at N.J.A.C. 5:23-3.14, effective September 6, 2022, with a six-month grace period (N.J.A.C. 5:23-1.6) allowing the prior code on complete applications. It governs a café's occupancy classification, means of egress, fire separation, and structural design.
businessImpact: A café's seating count, exit widths, restroom counts, and occupancy group (often A-2 if 50+ occupants or M if smaller) are all dictated by this subcode at design stage.
penalty: Permit denial / failed inspections; penalties under N.J.S.A. 52:27D-138 (up to $2,000 per violation).
recommendedAction: Confirm the design team classifies each store's occupancy group and egress under the 2021 NJ IBC before lease-signing to avoid costly redesign.

codeStandardName: New Jersey Barrier Free Subcode (IBC Chapter 11 Accessibility / ICC A117.1-2017)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州无障碍分规范（国际建筑规范第11章 / ICC A117.1-2017）
englishTitle: New Jersey Barrier Free Subcode (Accessibility, IBC Chapter 11)
agency: New Jersey Department of Community Affairs (DCA), Division of Codes and Standards
codeCitation: N.J.A.C. 5:23-3.14 (IBC Ch. 11); N.J.A.C. 5:23-7 (Barrier Free Subcode, directs to Ch. 11); ref. ICC A117.1-2017
coveredEntities: All newly constructed or altered public-accommodation/commercial buildings in NJ / 新泽西所有新建或改建的公共设施与商业建筑
keyRequirements: Requires accessible entrances, routes, restrooms, counters, and parking per IBC Chapter 11 using the technical provisions of ICC A117.1-2017 for new construction and alterations.
chineseSummary: 随2021年国际建筑规范的采用，新泽西无障碍规定并入建筑分规范第11章（5:23-3.14(b)），技术标准引用 ICC A117.1-2017；N.J.A.C. 5:23-7 现行条文指向第11章。该规范在建造阶段强制要求无障碍出入口、通道、洗手间、收银台高度及停车位，独立于联邦ADA并行适用。
englishSummary: With the 2021 IBC adoption, NJ's accessibility provisions sit in IBC Chapter 11 of the Building Subcode (N.J.A.C. 5:23-3.14(b)), using ICC A117.1-2017 as the technical standard; N.J.A.C. 5:23-7 still exists and now directs readers to Chapter 11. It mandates accessible entrances, routes, restrooms, counter heights, and parking at construction stage, applying in parallel with federal ADA.
businessImpact: Accessible service-counter height, restroom clearances, and an accessible entrance are mandatory in design — a missed dimension can block the certificate of occupancy.
penalty: Permit/CO denial; potential parallel exposure under ADA Title III and NJLAD public-accommodation claims.
recommendedAction: Have the architect certify A117.1-2017 compliance (counter heights, turning radii, restroom clearances) on plans before construction.

codeStandardName: Certificate of Occupancy and Change of Use Requirements (UCC)
chineseTitle: 入住证及用途变更要求（统一建筑规范）
englishTitle: Certificate of Occupancy and Change of Use Requirements
agency: New Jersey Department of Community Affairs (DCA); local construction officials
coveredEntities: Any party occupying a new building or changing the use of an existing space / 任何入住新建建筑或变更现有空间用途的主体
keyRequirements: Prohibits occupancy of new construction, and any change of use of an existing space, without first obtaining a certificate of occupancy after passing required inspections.
chineseSummary: N.J.A.C. 5:23-2.23 规定建筑须取得入住证后方可使用；5:23-2.6 规定变更用途须先申请并获得入住证，且须符合 5:23-6 翻修分规范。将零售铺位改为咖啡店通常构成用途变更，须重新办理入住证。
englishSummary: N.J.A.C. 5:23-2.23 bars occupancy without a certificate of occupancy after inspections, and 5:23-2.6 bars any change of use without first obtaining a new CO and complying with the Rehabilitation Subcode (5:23-6). Converting a retail space into a café is typically a change of use triggering a new CO.
businessImpact: A Luckin store cannot legally open or serve customers until the local construction official issues the certificate of occupancy for its specific use.
penalty: Occupancy without a CO is a violation under N.J.S.A. 52:27D-138 (up to $2,000 per violation) and can force closure.
recommendedAction: Build the CO/change-of-use inspection timeline into store-opening schedules and do not announce an opening date until the CO is in hand.

codeStandardName: New Jersey Uniform Fire Code — Registration & Inspection and Fire Safety Permits (2018 IFC adoption)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州统一消防规范——登记与检查及消防安全许可（采用2018年国际消防规范）
englishTitle: New Jersey Uniform Fire Code — Registration, Inspection & Permits
agency: New Jersey Department of Community Affairs (DCA), Division of Fire Safety; local fire officials
coveredEntities: All non-residential buildings in NJ, including cafés and mercantile occupancies / 新泽西所有非住宅建筑，含咖啡店及商业用途
keyRequirements: Requires every non-residential building to register with the local fire official and undergo periodic fire-safety inspections, with life-hazard uses paying annual registration and held to the NJ-amended 2018 IFC standards (citations under the 2018 IFC from 10/15/2024).
chineseSummary: 新泽西消防规范要求所有非住宅建筑向地方消防官登记并接受定期消防检查；列为“生命危害用途”者须年度登记并缴费。自2024年10月15日起，违规依经修订的2018年国际消防规范开具（此前依2015年版）。咖啡店须取得相关消防安全许可（如设有灭火系统的烹饪设备）。
englishSummary: The NJ Uniform Fire Code (N.J.A.C. 5:70) requires all non-residential buildings to register with the local fire official and undergo periodic fire-safety inspections; life-hazard uses pay annual registration fees. From October 15, 2024, violations are cited under the NJ-amended 2018 International Fire Code (previously the 2015 IFC). Cafés may also need fire-safety permits (e.g., for cooking equipment requiring suppression).
businessImpact: Each NJ store must register, pass annual fire inspections, and maintain extinguishers/exits in working order or face fines and possible closure orders.
inspectionCitationRecord: Common citations: blocked/locked exits, missing or untagged extinguishers, failure to register a non-residential use, and obstructed egress.
penalty: Penalties up to $5,000 per day per violation under the Uniform Fire Safety Act (N.J.S.A. 52:27D-192 et seq.); registration fees roughly $108–$749/yr by type.
recommendedAction: Register every NJ location as a non-residential use, schedule annual fire inspections, and keep extinguishers tagged and exits clear at all times.

codeStandardName: New Jersey Mechanical Subcode (2021 IMC) — Commercial Kitchen Exhaust
chineseTitle: 新泽西州机械分规范（2021年国际机械规范）——商用厨房排风
englishTitle: New Jersey Mechanical Subcode (2021 IMC) — Commercial Kitchen Exhaust
agency: New Jersey Department of Community Affairs (DCA), Division of Codes and Standards
coveredEntities: Commercial food establishments with grease-producing cooking and exhaust hoods / 设有产油烹饪及排油烟罩的商业餐饮场所
keyRequirements: Requires commercial kitchen exhaust hoods, grease ducts, and exhaust equipment to be designed and constructed per IMC 2021 Sections 506–509 for the type of cooking appliance served.
chineseSummary: N.J.A.C. 5:23-3.20 采用2021年国际机械规范作为机械分规范，自2022年9月6日生效。商用厨房排油烟系统须按第506至509条设计建造，油烟罩、排油烟管及排风设备须与所服务的烹饪设备类型匹配。
englishSummary: N.J.A.C. 5:23-3.20 adopts the 2021 IMC as the Mechanical Subcode, effective September 6, 2022. Commercial kitchen exhaust systems, grease ducts, and exhaust equipment must be designed per IMC Sections 506–509 for the cooking appliances served.
businessImpact: If a Luckin location does any grease-producing cooking (e.g., hot food, certain baking), it must install code-compliant Type I/II hoods and grease ducts permitted under the mechanical subcode.
penalty: Failed mechanical inspection / CO denial; UCC penalties up to $2,000 per violation.
recommendedAction: Confirm with the mechanical engineer whether any menu items trigger Type I hood/grease-duct requirements before equipment is ordered.

codeStandardName: NJDEP Safe Drinking Water Act — Cross-Connection Control / Backflow Prevention
chineseTitle: 新泽西州环保署《安全饮用水法》——交叉连接控制 / 防回流
englishTitle: NJDEP Safe Drinking Water Act — Backflow Prevention
agency: New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (NJDEP), Bureau of Safe Drinking Water; coordinated with UCC Plumbing Subcode
coveredEntities: Commercial water customers with cross-connection risk (food service, espresso/coffee equipment) / 存在交叉连接风险的商业用水户（餐饮、咖啡设备）
keyRequirements: Requires installation, permitting, and annual testing of approved backflow prevention assemblies (double check valve or reduced-pressure zone) on services with cross-connection risk.
chineseSummary: N.J.A.C. 7:10-10 与统一建筑规范管道分规范（5:23-3.15）共同要求存在交叉连接风险的设施安装经核准的防回流装置（双止回阀或减压区式），并需办理许可及每年测试。咖啡机、制冰机等设备的供水连接常触发此要求，以防止污染公共饮用水。
englishSummary: N.J.A.C. 7:10-10, working with the UCC Plumbing Subcode (5:23-3.15), requires facilities with cross-connection risk to install approved backflow prevention assemblies (double check valve or reduced-pressure zone), with permits and annual testing (results to the purveyor within 30 days). Espresso machines, ice makers, and similar equipment commonly trigger this to protect the public water supply.
businessImpact: Coffee/espresso and ice equipment typically requires a tested backflow preventer; missing or untested devices can prompt water-utility shutoff notices.
penalty: Water purveyor may terminate service for non-compliance; NJDEP SDWA penalties for violations.
recommendedAction: Install and annually test certified backflow preventers on all beverage/ice water connections and retain the test reports for the water utility.

codeStandardName: New Jersey Smoke-Free Air Act — No-Smoking Signage
chineseTitle: 新泽西州《无烟空气法》——禁烟标识
englishTitle: New Jersey Smoke-Free Air Act — No-Smoking Signage
agency: New Jersey Department of Health (and local boards of health)
codeCitation: N.J.S.A. 26:3D-55 et seq. (statutory posting duty N.J.S.A. 26:3D-61); N.J.A.C. 8:6
coveredEntities: Persons in control of indoor public places and workplaces, including cafés / 室内公共场所及工作场所的管控人，含咖啡店
keyRequirements: Requires the person in control of an indoor public place/workplace to post clearly visible no-smoking signs (indicating violators are subject to a fine) at every public entrance.
chineseSummary: 《无烟空气法》于2006年1月15日签署，第90日（约2006年4月）起使全州室内公共场所与工作场所禁烟。N.J.S.A. 26:3D-61 及 N.J.A.C. 8:6 要求在每个公共入口张贴清晰可见、字体颜色对比明显的禁烟标识，并注明违者将被处罚款。后续修订将电子烟纳入管制范围。
englishSummary: The Smoke-Free Air Act (signed January 15, 2006; effective on the 90th day, ~April 2006) made indoor public places and workplaces statewide smoke-free. N.J.S.A. 26:3D-61 and N.J.A.C. 8:6 require clearly visible, color-contrasting no-smoking signs (stating violators are subject to a fine) at every public entrance. Later amendments extended coverage to electronic smoking devices.
businessImpact: Each NJ café must post compliant no-smoking signage at entrances or risk local board-of-health fines during inspection.
inspectionCitationRecord: Fines under N.J.S.A. 26:3D-58 run $250 (first offense), $500 (second), $1,000 (each subsequent) for both smokers and non-compliant establishments.
penalty: Establishment penalties for failure to post/enforce: $250 first offense, $500 second, $1,000 each subsequent under N.J.S.A. 26:3D-58.
recommendedAction: Post NJDOH-format no-smoking signs at every public entrance of each NJ store and include vaping in the prohibition.

codeStandardName: ADA Title I (Employment) and NJ Law Against Discrimination — Disability Accommodation
chineseTitle: 《美国残疾人法》第一篇（就业）与新泽西《反歧视法》——残疾人合理便利
englishTitle: ADA Title I (Employment) & NJ Law Against Discrimination
agency: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC); NJ Division on Civil Rights (NJLAD)
codeCitation: 42 U.S.C. 12111 et seq. (ADA Title I); N.J.S.A. 10:5-1 et seq. (NJLAD)
coveredEntities: Employers with 15+ employees (ADA Title I); ALL NJ employers regardless of size (NJLAD) / 15名以上员工的雇主（ADA第一篇）；新泽西所有规模雇主（NJLAD）
keyRequirements: Requires employers to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified employees/applicants with disabilities absent undue hardship — and under NJLAD this duty applies to NJ employers of any size.
chineseSummary: ADA第一篇要求15名以上员工的雇主为合格残疾员工提供合理便利（除非造成过度负担）。新泽西《反歧视法》（N.J.S.A. 10:5）范围更广，适用于所有规模的雇主（最少一名员工）——即便员工不足15人，也须进行互动程序评估合理便利。两者对咖啡店招聘与用工并行适用。
englishSummary: ADA Title I requires employers with 15+ employees to reasonably accommodate qualified employees and applicants with disabilities absent undue hardship. The NJLAD (N.J.S.A. 10:5) is broader and applies to NJ employers of any size (as few as one employee), requiring the interactive accommodation process even below 15 employees. Both apply in parallel to café hiring and staffing.
businessImpact: As Luckin staffs NJ stores, both ADA and NJLAD require an interactive accommodation process for disabled baristas/applicants — and NJLAD applies even to a single-store operation.
inspectionCitationRecord: EEOC charges and NJ DCR complaints commonly arise from failure to engage in the interactive process or denial of accommodation.
penalty: ADA: compensatory/punitive damages capped by employer size; NJLAD: uncapped compensatory and punitive damages plus attorneys' fees.
recommendedAction: Train NJ store managers to engage the interactive accommodation process for any disability request, applying NJLAD's any-size threshold.

codeStandardName: Proposed Adoption of 2024 ICC Model Codes (2024 IBC/IMC/IPC-NSPC/IECC, 2023 NEC) into the UCC
chineseTitle: 拟采用2024年ICC示范规范（2024年IBC/IMC/IPC-NSPC/IECC、2023年NEC）纳入统一建筑规范
englishTitle: Proposed Adoption of 2024 ICC Model Codes into the NJ UCC
agency: New Jersey Department of Community Affairs (DCA), Division of Codes and Standards
codeCitation: Proposed amendments to N.J.A.C. 5:23-3.14, 3.15, 3.16, 3.17, 3.18, 3.20, 3.21, 3.22 (PRN 2025-136; published in the October 20, 2025 N.J.R.; filed Sept. 19, 2025)
coveredEntities: All future NJ construction and alteration projects once adopted / 一经采用，适用于新泽西所有未来的建造与改建项目
keyRequirements: Would update NJ's building, plumbing, mechanical, fuel-gas, energy, and electrical subcodes to the 2024 ICC family and 2023 NEC, modifying current design standards once effective.
chineseSummary: 社区事务部以提案号 PRN 2025-136（2025年9月19日报送、刊于2025年10月20日《新泽西州公报》）提出拟将统一建筑规范各分规范更新为2024年版IBC、IRC、IECC、IMC、IFGC、NSPC及2023年版NEC，公众意见征询截至2025年12月19日。该规则尚处提案阶段、尚未生效，但将影响今后新店设计标准。
englishSummary: By proposal PRN 2025-136 (filed September 19, 2025; published in the October 20, 2025 New Jersey Register), DCA proposed amending the UCC subcodes to adopt the 2024 IBC, IRC, IECC, IMC, IFGC, and NSPC plus the 2023 NEC, with a public comment deadline of December 19, 2025. The rule is still proposed and not yet effective, but will reset design standards for future stores once adopted.
businessImpact: Luckin's NJ store prototypes and MEP designs will need to migrate to the 2024 codes once this rulemaking is finalized, affecting energy and ventilation specs.
recommendedAction: Track the DCA rulemaking (PRN 2025-136) and have the design team ready to update store prototypes to the 2024 ICC codes once an effective date is set.

codeStandardName: Federal OSHA Coverage of Private-Sector NJ Employers (PEOSH State Plan covers public sector only)
chineseTitle: 联邦OSHA对新泽西私营雇主的管辖（PEOSH州计划仅覆盖公共部门）
englishTitle: Federal OSHA Coverage of Private-Sector NJ Employers
agency: Federal Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA); NJ PEOSH (public sector only)
codeCitation: 29 U.S.C. 651 et seq. (OSH Act); 29 CFR 1903 (inspections); NJ PEOSH under N.J.S.A. 34:6A-25 et seq.
coveredEntities: Private-sector employers in NJ (e.g., a coffee chain) are under FEDERAL OSHA; NJ PEOSH covers only state/local government employees / 新泽西私营雇主（如咖啡连锁）受联邦OSHA管辖；PEOSH仅覆盖州及地方政府雇员
keyRequirements: Confirms that a private café in NJ is regulated and inspected by federal OSHA (not the NJ state plan), so federal 29 CFR standards and the General Duty Clause apply directly.
chineseSummary: 新泽西的 PEOSH 州计划仅覆盖州及地方政府雇员，不覆盖私营部门。私营咖啡连锁店在新泽西由联邦 OSHA 直接管辖与检查，适用联邦29 CFR标准及《一般责任条款》。这一管辖归属决定了合规对标对象为联邦OSHA而非州计划。
englishSummary: New Jersey's PEOSH State Plan covers only state and local government employees, not the private sector. A private coffee chain in NJ is therefore regulated and inspected directly by federal OSHA, with the federal 29 CFR standards and the General Duty Clause applying. This jurisdictional split determines that Luckin benchmarks to federal OSHA, not the NJ state plan.
businessImpact: Luckin's NJ workplace-safety compliance is benchmarked to federal OSHA standards and federal inspectors, not a state OSHA plan — important for consistent multi-state programs.
inspectionCitationRecord: Federal OSHA conducts NJ private-sector inspections; NJ also offers free 21(d) on-site consultation to private employers.
penalty: Federal OSHA penalties (2026): up to $16,550 per serious violation and $165,514 per willful/repeat violation.
recommendedAction: Apply federal OSHA standards to all NJ stores and consider NJ's free Section 21(d) on-site consultation for a no-penalty compliance check.

codeStandardName: Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP)
chineseTitle: 工伤与职业病预防计划（IIPP）
englishTitle: Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP)
agency: Cal/OSHA — California Division of Occupational Safety and Health (DIR)
coveredEntities: Every California employer / 加州所有雇主（含每一家咖啡门店）
keyRequirements: Every employer must establish, implement, and maintain a written IIPP covering responsibility, hazard identification and inspections, hazard correction, injury investigation, employee safety communication, and training.
chineseSummary: 加州自1991年起要求每一家雇主建立并维护书面的工伤与职业病预防计划（IIPP），内容须包括责任人、危害识别与定期检查、危害纠正、事故调查、员工安全沟通及培训。该计划是Cal/OSHA检查时的首要核查项，缺失或不完整属最常见的引用违规。
englishSummary: Since 1991, California requires every employer to maintain a written IIPP that designates responsibility, identifies and corrects workplace hazards through periodic inspections, investigates injuries, communicates with employees on safety, and trains workers. It is the cornerstone of Cal/OSHA enforcement and the single most commonly cited standard in the state.
businessImpact: An incomplete or missing IIPP is the most frequently cited Cal/OSHA violation and is checked first in any café inspection, so every Luckin location must have a current written program on site.
inspectionCitationRecord: 8 CCR 3203 is consistently Cal/OSHA's most-cited standard year over year across all industries, including food service.
penalty: General/regulatory violations typically up to ~$15,873 each; willful/repeat up to ~$158,727 (2024 Cal/OSHA maximums).
recommendedAction: Adopt a written IIPP for each store, assign a named responsible person, and document periodic inspections, hazard corrections, and employee safety training.

codeStandardName: Workplace Violence Prevention Plan (SB 553)
chineseTitle: 工作场所暴力预防计划（SB 553）
englishTitle: Workplace Violence Prevention Plan (SB 553)
agency: Cal/OSHA — California Division of Occupational Safety and Health (DIR)
coveredEntities: Most California employers and employees; limited exception for worksites with fewer than 10 employees at a time not open to the public / 加州绝大多数雇主与员工；少于10名在场员工且不向公众开放的场所有限豁免
keyRequirements: Employers must establish a written Workplace Violence Prevention Plan, maintain a violent-incident log, provide annual employee training, and record/investigate workplace violence incidents.
chineseSummary: 自2024年7月1日起，加州劳工法第6401.9条要求绝大多数雇主制定并实施书面的工作场所暴力预防计划（WVPP），建立暴力事件记录簿，并至少每年对员工进行培训。咖啡门店面向公众、常涉现金交易，属典型适用对象；Cal/OSHA正在制定到2026年12月31日须完成的通用行业正式标准。
englishSummary: Effective July 1, 2024, Labor Code § 6401.9 requires most California employers to implement a written Workplace Violence Prevention Plan, keep a violent-incident log, and train employees at least annually. Public-facing, cash-handling cafés are squarely covered; Cal/OSHA must finalize a formal general-industry standard by December 31, 2026.
businessImpact: Public-facing coffee shops handling cash are prime targets, so each location must maintain a tailored WVPP and incident log or face Cal/OSHA citations.
inspectionCitationRecord: Cal/OSHA began enforcing the WVPP requirement on July 1, 2024 and treats a missing written plan as a citable IIPP-related violation.
penalty: Cited as a serious/general violation; up to ~$15,873 per violation under 2024 Cal/OSHA maximums.
recommendedAction: Adopt a store-specific Workplace Violence Prevention Plan, train all staff annually, and start a violent-incident log immediately.

codeStandardName: Heat Illness Prevention in Indoor Places of Employment
chineseTitle: 室内工作场所高温疾病预防标准
englishTitle: Heat Illness Prevention in Indoor Places of Employment
agency: Cal/OSHA — California Division of Occupational Safety and Health (DIR)
coveredEntities: Most indoor workplaces where temperature reaches 82°F, including restaurants and cafés / 室内温度达82°F（约27.8°C）的大多数工作场所，含餐厅与咖啡店
keyRequirements: Where indoor temperature reaches 82°F employers must provide cool drinking water and cool-down areas; at 87°F (or near hot equipment) they must apply engineering/administrative controls and maintain a written indoor heat illness prevention plan.
chineseSummary: 加州8 CCR第3396条于2024年7月23日生效，是全美首部室内高温标准。当室内温度达82°F时，雇主须提供清凉饮用水与降温区；达87°F或员工靠近高温设备（如蒸汽棒、烤箱、洗碗机）时，须采取工程/管理控制措施并维护书面预防计划。咖啡门店厨房及出品区在夏季极易触发该阈值。
englishSummary: Cal/OSHA's 8 CCR § 3396, effective July 23, 2024, is the nation's first indoor heat standard. At 82°F employers must provide cool water and cool-down areas; at 87°F (or near radiant heat sources) they must add engineering/administrative controls and keep a written plan. Café kitchens and barista stations with espresso machines, ovens, and dishwashers readily hit these triggers.
businessImpact: Espresso steam, ovens, and dishwashers can push barista areas past 82–87°F, obligating cafés to supply water, cool-down zones, and a written heat plan.
inspectionCitationRecord: Standard took effect July 23, 2024 after OAL approval; Cal/OSHA enforces it under the same scheme as the outdoor heat standard (§ 3395).
penalty: Up to ~$15,873 per general violation; higher for serious, willful, or repeat under 2024 Cal/OSHA maximums.
recommendedAction: Measure peak indoor temperatures in kitchen/barista areas, install cooling or cool-down areas, and adopt a written indoor heat illness prevention plan.

codeStandardName: 2022 California Building Code, Title 24, Part 2
chineseTitle: 2022年版《加州建筑规范》（第24编第2部分）
englishTitle: 2022 California Building Code (Title 24, Part 2)
agency: California Building Standards Commission (BSC) / Dept. of General Services
coveredEntities: All new construction and tenant improvements permitted in California, including retail/food-service buildouts / 加州所有取得许可的新建及改建项目，含零售/餐饮装修
keyRequirements: All construction and tenant-improvement projects must comply with the 2022 CBC for structural, occupancy (Group A/B/M), egress, and fire/life-safety provisions, applied through local building permits and plan check.
chineseSummary: 2022年版《加州建筑规范》（基于2021 IBC）自2023年1月1日起对取得建筑许可的项目生效，按三年周期更新。注意：2025版已于2026年1月1日取代本版，2026年1月1日及以后递交许可申请的项目须按2025版执行。咖啡门店的店面装修须确定用途分类（通常为B或A-2集会用途）、疏散通道、占用人数及消防安全要求，并通过当地建筑部门的图纸审查与验收。
englishSummary: The 2022 California Building Code (based on the 2021 IBC) took effect January 1, 2023 on a three-year cycle and governed structural, occupancy, egress, and life-safety design for permitted projects. Note: it has been superseded by the 2025 edition for permit applications dated on or after January 1, 2026. A Luckin buildout must be classified (typically Group B, or A-2 if assembly occupancy is triggered) and pass local plan check and inspection.
businessImpact: Every new café buildout or remodel is plan-checked against the applicable CBC edition, so occupancy classification and egress design directly drive permit approval and opening timeline.
inspectionCitationRecord: Enforced by local building departments via plan check, permit inspections, and certificate-of-occupancy sign-off.
penalty: Local stop-work orders, permit denial, and re-inspection fees; varies by jurisdiction.
recommendedAction: Engage a California-licensed architect/engineer to design each buildout to the applicable code edition (2025 CBC for 2026 permits) and secure plan-check approval before construction.

codeStandardName: 2025 California Building Standards Code (Title 24)
chineseTitle: 2025年版《加州建筑标准规范》（第24编）
englishTitle: 2025 California Building Standards Code (Title 24)
agency: California Building Standards Commission (BSC) / Dept. of General Services
codeCitation: 24 CCR (Parts incl. Building, Fire, Plumbing, Mechanical, Electrical, Energy)
coveredEntities: All California building projects with a permit application dated on or after January 1, 2026 / 所有于2026年1月1日及以后递交许可申请的加州建设项目
keyRequirements: Projects applying for permits on or after January 1, 2026 must comply with the 2025 Title 24 edition (Building, Fire, Plumbing, Mechanical, Electrical, Energy and Green codes), superseding the 2022 edition.
chineseSummary: 2025年版《加州建筑标准规范》（第24编）于2025年7月1日公布，已于2026年1月1日生效，取代2022版。凡在该日期及之后递交许可申请的咖啡门店项目，须按2025版的建筑、消防、给排水、机械、电气与节能等分编进行设计。2026年开店的Luckin项目均适用本版。
englishSummary: The 2025 California Building Standards Code (Title 24) was published July 1, 2025 and became effective January 1, 2026, replacing the 2022 edition. Café projects whose permit applications are dated on or after that date must be designed to the 2025 Building, Fire, Plumbing, Mechanical, Electrical, Energy, and Green codes.
businessImpact: Any Luckin buildout permitted in 2026 must meet the 2025 code, so site-selection and design timing decide which edition (and which cost requirements) apply.
inspectionCitationRecord: Effective date confirmed by BSC publication and numerous local building department notices (e.g., Riverside County, City of Santa Ana).
penalty: Enforced via permit denial and stop-work orders for noncompliant designs.
recommendedAction: Design all 2026 store buildouts to the 2025 Title 24 edition and confirm the applicable code edition with the local building department at permit submittal.

codeStandardName: 2022 California Fire Code, Title 24, Part 9
chineseTitle: 2022年版《加州消防规范》（第24编第9部分）
englishTitle: 2022 California Fire Code (Title 24, Part 9)
agency: California Building Standards Commission / Office of the State Fire Marshal (CAL FIRE)
coveredEntities: Commercial occupancies including food-service facilities with commercial cooking operations / 商业用途场所，含设有商用烹饪设备的餐饮场所
keyRequirements: Commercial cooking operations producing grease-laden vapors must have a Type I hood with a UL 300-listed automatic fire-extinguishing system, plus required portable extinguishers, exit/egress maintenance, and fire department access per the 2022 CFC.
chineseSummary: 2022年版《加州消防规范》（基于2021 IFC）自2023年1月1日起生效，由州消防局与地方消防部门执行（2025版已于2026年1月1日取代本版）。产生含油脂蒸气的商用烹饪须配Type I排烟罩及符合UL 300的自动灭火系统；门店还须维护疏散通道、配备便携灭火器并接受年度消防检查。即便Luckin以冲泡饮品为主，若有烤制或加热设备仍可能触发相关要求。
englishSummary: The 2022 California Fire Code (based on the 2021 IFC) took effect January 1, 2023 and is enforced by the State Fire Marshal and local fire departments (superseded by the 2025 edition for 2026 permits). Grease-producing commercial cooking requires a Type I hood with a UL 300 automatic suppression system, and all occupancies must maintain extinguishers, exits, and fire-department access. Even beverage-led cafés can trigger these provisions if they bake or heat food.
businessImpact: If a Luckin location adds ovens or grease-producing equipment, it must install and annually service a Type I hood suppression system to pass fire inspection and keep its operating permit.
inspectionCitationRecord: Enforced through local fire department annual operational permits and inspections; failure can suspend an operating permit.
penalty: Operating-permit suspension, re-inspection fees, and local administrative fines; varies by jurisdiction.
recommendedAction: Assess whether cooking equipment triggers Type I hood/suppression requirements and schedule semiannual UL 300 system servicing and annual fire inspections.

codeStandardName: CBC Chapter 11B — Accessible Parking Identification Signage
chineseTitle: 《加州建筑规范》第11B章——无障碍停车标识
englishTitle: CBC Chapter 11B — Accessible Parking Identification Signage
agency: Division of the State Architect (DSA) / California Building Standards Commission
coveredEntities: Public accommodations and commercial facilities providing parking, including coffee shops with parking / 提供停车位的公共设施与商业场所，含设停车位的咖啡店
keyRequirements: Each accessible parking space must have a reflectorized sign (min. 70 sq in) bearing the International Symbol of Accessibility and the words 'Minimum Fine $250,' with a 'Van Accessible' designation at van spaces, mounted 60 in min. above grade.
chineseSummary: 加州《建筑规范》第11B-502.6条对无障碍停车标识的要求严于联邦ADA：每个无障碍车位须设反光标志（不小于70平方英寸），含国际无障碍标志及'Minimum Fine $250'字样，厢式车位另加'Van Accessible'标识，底边离地不低于60英寸。仅有ADA标志而缺少'$250最低罚款'字样在加州即属违规，是无障碍诉讼的高发点。
englishSummary: California's CBC § 11B-502.6 is stricter than federal ADA: every accessible stall needs a reflectorized sign (min. 70 sq in) with the International Symbol of Accessibility and the words 'Minimum Fine $250,' a 'Van Accessible' tag at van stalls, mounted at least 60 in above grade. A sign that omits the '$250' language complies with the ADA but violates California law and is a frequent driver of access lawsuits.
businessImpact: Cafés with their own parking are common targets of California accessibility lawsuits, so missing the '$250' wording or van designation can trigger statutory damages of at least $4,000 per offense.
inspectionCitationRecord: Frequently cited in California construction-related accessibility (Unruh/CDPA) demand letters and lawsuits and in CASp inspection reports.
penalty: Statutory damages of at least $4,000 per violation under the Unruh Civil Act (Civil Code § 52); plus plaintiff's attorney fees.
recommendedAction: Audit every accessible parking sign for the '$250' wording, van designation, reflectivity, and height; correct deficiencies before opening.

codeStandardName: Construction-Related Accessibility Standards Compliance Act (CASp / Disability Access Notice)
chineseTitle: 施工相关无障碍标准合规法（CASp / 无障碍通行告知）
englishTitle: Construction-Related Accessibility Standards Compliance Act (CASp / Disability Access Notice)
agency: California Commission on Disability Access / Division of the State Architect (CASp program)
codeCitation: Civil Code §§ 55.51–55.545 (CASp report § 55.53; court stay & reduced damages § 55.54); Civil Code § 1938 (lease CASp disclosure)
coveredEntities: Owners and tenants of places of public accommodation in California; commercial landlords must disclose CASp status in leases / 加州公共accommodation场所的业主与租户；商业出租人须在租约中披露CASp状态
keyRequirements: Commercial leases must state whether the premises have been CASp-inspected, and a voluntary pre-litigation CASp inspection can grant a defendant a court stay and early evaluation conference plus reduced statutory damages in access lawsuits.
chineseSummary: 加州民法典第55.53/55.54及1938条构成的CASp（持证无障碍专家）制度，要求商业租约披露场所是否经CASp检查（§1938）。门店若主动取得CASp检查报告（§55.53）并保留记录，在遭遇无障碍诉讼时可依§55.54获得法院暂缓审理、提前评估会议及最低法定赔偿减免（在符合条件且60天内整改时可降至$1,000/次）。这是加州咖啡门店降低Unruh法案诉讼风险的关键工具。
englishSummary: California's CASp program under Civil Code §§ 55.53, 55.54 and 1938 requires commercial leases to disclose whether the premises were inspected by a Certified Access Specialist. A business that obtains and retains a CASp inspection report (§ 55.53) gains, in an access lawsuit, a right under § 55.54 to a court stay, an early evaluation conference, and reduced minimum statutory damages (as low as $1,000 per offense where conditions are met and violations are corrected within 60 days). It is California cafés' key tool to limit Unruh Act litigation exposure.
businessImpact: A CASp inspection sharply reduces a café's exposure to California's frequent 'drive-by' accessibility lawsuits and is increasingly required disclosure in commercial leases.
inspectionCitationRecord: Voluntary program; CASp-inspected status is asserted as a defense in numerous Unruh Civil Rights Act / CDPA access cases to obtain stays and reduced damages.
penalty: Without CASp protections, Unruh damages are min. $4,000 per offense; CASp status can reduce minimum statutory damages to $1,000 per offense.
recommendedAction: Commission a CASp inspection for each leased store, retain the report, and confirm the lease contains the required Civil Code § 1938 CASp disclosure.

codeStandardName: CALGreen — California Green Building Standards Code (Title 24, Part 11)
chineseTitle: CALGreen——《加州绿色建筑标准规范》（第24编第11部分）
englishTitle: CALGreen — California Green Building Standards Code (Title 24, Part 11)
agency: California Building Standards Commission (BSC) / Dept. of General Services
codeCitation: 24 CCR Part 11 (CALGreen), Chapter 5 Nonresidential Mandatory Measures
coveredEntities: Newly constructed and altered nonresidential buildings, including retail/food-service tenant improvements / 新建及改建的非住宅建筑，含零售/餐饮租户装修
keyRequirements: Nonresidential projects must meet CALGreen mandatory measures for water-efficient fixtures, construction waste diversion, and indoor air quality; embodied-carbon limits (since July 1, 2024) apply only to large projects (nonresidential over 100,000 sf, schools over 50,000 sf).
chineseSummary: CALGreen（第24编第11部分）是全美首部强制性州级绿色建筑规范，2022版自2023年1月1日起对非住宅项目生效。咖啡门店装修须满足节水洁具、施工废弃物减量、室内空气质量等强制条款；自2024年7月1日起的隐含碳排放控制要求仅适用于较大项目（非住宅超10万平方英尺、学校超5万平方英尺），普通咖啡门店一般不触发。该规范在建筑许可图纸审查阶段一并核查。
englishSummary: CALGreen (Title 24, Part 11) is the nation's first mandatory statewide green building code; the 2022 edition took effect January 1, 2023 for nonresidential projects. Café buildouts must meet mandatory measures for water-efficient fixtures, construction-waste diversion, and indoor air quality. The embodied-carbon measures effective July 1, 2024 apply only to large projects (nonresidential over 100,000 sf, schools over 50,000 sf), which a typical café would not trigger. Compliance is verified during building-permit plan check.
businessImpact: Café buildout drawings must include a CALGreen checklist to obtain a building permit, affecting fixture, ventilation, and waste-management specifications; embodied-carbon rules generally do not apply at café scale.
inspectionCitationRecord: Enforced through local building department plan check via the required CALGreen mandatory-measures checklist and field verification.
penalty: Permit denial and corrective re-inspection; varies by jurisdiction.
recommendedAction: Include a completed CALGreen nonresidential mandatory-measures checklist with each permit submittal and specify compliant fixtures and ventilation.

codeStandardName: Certificate of Occupancy / Change of Occupancy (CBC Section 111)
chineseTitle: 占用许可证 / 用途变更（CBC 第111节）
englishTitle: Certificate of Occupancy / Change of Occupancy (CBC Section 111)
agency: Local building departments (per California Building Code)
