coveredEntities: Any building or tenant space newly occupied, changed in use/occupancy, or with a new tenant / 任何新占用、用途/占用分类变更或更换租户的建筑或租户空间
keyRequirements: A building or tenant space may not be lawfully occupied until the building official issues a Certificate of Occupancy, and each change of occupancy, use, or tenancy generally requires a new Certificate of Occupancy after inspection.
chineseSummary: 根据《加州建筑规范》第111节，建筑或租户空间在取得建筑官员签发的占用许可证（C of O）前不得合法使用；用途、占用分类或租户的每次变更通常都须经检查后重新签发占用许可证。Luckin进驻既有店面（如从前零售改为餐饮A-2/B用途）几乎都会触发新占用许可证及相应消防、无障碍、机电验收。
englishSummary: Under CBC § 111, a building or tenant space cannot be lawfully occupied until the building official issues a Certificate of Occupancy, and each change of use, occupancy classification, or tenancy generally requires a new certificate after inspection. A Luckin taking over an existing storefront (e.g., a former retail space converted to A-2/B food use) almost always triggers a new C of O and accompanying fire, accessibility, and MEP sign-offs.
businessImpact: A store legally cannot open to customers until it receives its Certificate of Occupancy, making C of O timing a gating item for every new location's launch date.
inspectionCitationRecord: Enforced by local building officials; occupying without a valid C of O can result in red-tag/stop-use orders.
penalty: Stop-use/closure orders and per-day administrative penalties; varies by jurisdiction.
recommendedAction: Confirm the required occupancy classification early and sequence build-out, MEP, fire, and accessibility inspections to obtain the C of O before the planned opening date.

codeStandardName: Cross-Connection Control Policy Handbook (Backflow Prevention)
chineseTitle: 交叉连接控制政策手册（防回流）
englishTitle: Cross-Connection Control Policy Handbook (Backflow Prevention)
agency: State Water Resources Control Board — Division of Drinking Water
codeCitation: Cross-Connection Control Policy Handbook (CCCPH), superseding 17 CCR §§ 7583–7605
coveredEntities: Water customers with cross-connection hazards, including food-service facilities with espresso machines, carbonators, and grease/utility connections / 存在交叉连接危害的用水客户，含配有咖啡机、碳酸化设备及给排水连接的餐饮场所
keyRequirements: Customers posing a contamination hazard must install and annually test an approved backflow prevention assembly, with new/replacement commercial assemblies installed above ground as of July 1, 2024.
chineseSummary: 加州水资源控制委员会的《交叉连接控制政策手册》（CCCPH）于2024年7月1日生效，取代原《加州法规第17编》的防回流规定（手册于2023年12月19日通过，2025年6月又作修订延长部分期限）。存在污染风险的用水客户须安装经认证的防回流装置并每年测试；自2024年7月1日起，因产权、用途变更或建筑改造新装或更换的商用防回流装置须安装在地面以上。咖啡机、碳酸/苏打水设备等是典型的交叉连接危害源。
englishSummary: The State Water Board's Cross-Connection Control Policy Handbook (CCCPH) took effect July 1, 2024, replacing the former Title 17 backflow regulations (adopted Dec 19, 2023; revised June 2025 to extend certain deadlines). Customers posing a contamination hazard must install and annually test an approved backflow prevention assembly, and as of July 1, 2024 new or replacement commercial assemblies (on change of ownership, use, or building modification) must be installed above ground. Espresso machines and carbonators are classic cross-connection hazards in cafés.
businessImpact: Espresso and carbonated-beverage equipment create cross-connection hazards, so each café must install and annually test a compliant backflow assembly to keep its water service and health permit.
inspectionCitationRecord: Enforced by local water purveyors and county environmental health; failed/overdue annual backflow tests can trigger water-service shutoff notices.
penalty: Water-service termination for noncompliance; local purveyor administrative fees vary.
recommendedAction: Inventory beverage/utility connections, install required backflow assemblies, and schedule certified annual testing with records kept for the local water purveyor.

codeStandardName: 780 CMR — Massachusetts State Building Code, 10th Edition (base code: 2021 IBC with MA amendments)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州建筑规范 780 CMR 第十版（以2021版IBC为基础并附州修订）
englishTitle: Massachusetts State Building Code, 780 CMR, 10th Edition
agency: Board of Building Regulations and Standards (BBRS), Department of Public Safety / Office of Public Safety and Inspections
codeCitation: 780 CMR (10th Edition); based on 2021 International Building Code (IBC)
coveredEntities: All new construction, alterations, change of use and occupancy in MA, including commercial/mercantile and Business/Assembly tenant fit-outs such as cafés / 马萨诸塞州所有新建、改建、用途变更工程，包括咖啡店等商业/集会类租户装修
keyRequirements: New building/alteration permits must comply with the 10th Edition (2021 IBC-based) requirements for occupancy classification, means of egress, structural, and fit-out; it is the sole enforceable code for permit applications filed after June 30, 2025.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州建筑规范第十版以2021版国际建筑规范（IBC）为基础并附州修订，于2024年10月11日生效，自2025年6月30日后提交的许可申请起成为唯一可执行版本。咖啡店的新店选址、装修、用途变更（如零售转餐饮/集会用途）须按本规范取得建筑许可并通过验收。
englishSummary: The 10th Edition of 780 CMR, based on the 2021 IBC with Massachusetts amendments, took effect October 11, 2024 and is the sole enforceable code for permit applications filed after June 30, 2025. Any new Luckin store build-out, tenant alteration, or change of use (e.g., retail to food/assembly) must be permitted and inspected under this edition.
businessImpact: Every new store fit-out and any material renovation in MA must be designed and permitted to the 10th Edition, affecting design fees, build timelines, and occupancy classification.
penalty: Stop-work orders, permit denial/revocation, and fines under M.G.L. c. 143; occupancy withheld until compliance.
recommendedAction: Confirm all MA store designs and tenant build-outs are permitted under the 780 CMR 10th Edition (2021 IBC) and budget design/permit timelines accordingly.

codeStandardName: 527 CMR 1.00 — Massachusetts Comprehensive Fire Safety Code (based on NFPA 1, 2021 Edition with MA amendments)
chineseTitle: 527 CMR 1.00 马萨诸塞州综合消防安全规范（基于2021版NFPA 1并附州修订）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Comprehensive Fire Safety Code, 527 CMR 1.00
agency: Board of Fire Prevention Regulations (BFPR) / Department of Fire Services; enforced by local fire department head (AHJ)
codeCitation: 527 CMR 1.00 (adopting NFPA 1, 2021 Edition, as modified by 1.05)
coveredEntities: All buildings and premises in MA including retail food establishments and limited-service restaurants/cafés / 马萨诸塞州所有建筑与场所，包括零售食品店及有限服务餐饮/咖啡店
keyRequirements: Establishments must maintain fire protection systems, egress, extinguishers, hood/duct suppression where applicable, and obtain required fire permits and inspections from the local AHJ under the 2021 NFPA 1-based code.
chineseSummary: 527 CMR 1.00 采用2021版NFPA 1并附马萨诸塞州修订，现行版本于2022年12月9日生效，由州消防局与地方消防部门（管辖机关AHJ）执行。咖啡店须维护灭火器、疏散通道、必要的厨房抑制系统，并取得地方消防许可与年检合格。
englishSummary: 527 CMR 1.00 adopts NFPA 1 (2021 Edition) with Massachusetts amendments; the current edition is effective December 9, 2022 and is enforced by the local fire chief as Authority Having Jurisdiction. Cafés must keep extinguishers, egress, and any required suppression systems compliant and pass local fire inspections.
businessImpact: Failed fire inspection can block or revoke a certificate of occupancy and shut a store; ongoing compliance is required for every MA location.
penalty: Fines and orders under M.G.L. c. 148; the AHJ may order correction, deny permits, or close premises for serious fire-code violations.
recommendedAction: Schedule local fire-department plan review and inspection for each MA store and maintain a recurring fire-system inspection/permit calendar.

codeStandardName: 527 CMR 1.00 — Next Edition Adopting NFPA 1, 2024 Edition (proposed Massachusetts amendments)
chineseTitle: 527 CMR 1.00 下一版（拟采用2024版NFPA 1并附州修订）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Comprehensive Fire Safety Code — Forthcoming Edition (NFPA 1, 2024)
agency: Board of Fire Prevention Regulations (BFPR) / Department of Fire Services
coveredEntities: All MA buildings and premises including limited-service restaurants/cafés / 马萨诸塞州所有建筑与场所，包括有限服务餐饮/咖啡店
keyRequirements: The BFPR is developing the next fire code edition adopting the 2024 NFPA 1 with state amendments; a draft-amendment vote was scheduled for April 2, 2026 with publication anticipated as soon as summer 2026 (not yet effective).
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州消防预防规章委员会正在制定下一版消防规范，拟采用2024版NFPA 1并附州修订；修订草案表决定于2026年4月2日，预计最快2026年夏季发布，目前尚未生效。新版可能更新灭火器、抑制系统及危险品要求，咖啡店应提前关注。
englishSummary: The Board of Fire Prevention Regulations is preparing the next edition of 527 CMR 1.00 to adopt NFPA 1 (2024 Edition) with Massachusetts amendments; a draft-amendment vote was set for April 2, 2026 with publication anticipated as soon as summer 2026. It is not yet effective, but will eventually replace the 2021-based code for new permits.
businessImpact: Upcoming fire-code changes may alter suppression, extinguisher, and storage requirements for future store build-outs, so design standards should track the pending edition.
penalty: N/A until adopted; once effective, the same M.G.L. c. 148 enforcement and AHJ orders will apply.
recommendedAction: Monitor BFPR rulemaking for the NFPA 1 (2024) adoption and have design/MEP partners flag any changes affecting future MA fit-outs before publication.

codeStandardName: 521 CMR — Massachusetts Architectural Access Board (AAB) Rules and Regulations
chineseTitle: 521 CMR 马萨诸塞州建筑无障碍委员会（AAB）规章
englishTitle: Massachusetts Architectural Access Board Regulations, 521 CMR
agency: Massachusetts Architectural Access Board (AAB), Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation; enforced by local building inspectors
coveredEntities: All buildings open to the public in MA, including restaurants and retail establishments / 马萨诸塞州所有向公众开放的建筑，包括餐厅和零售店
keyRequirements: Public accommodations must meet MA accessibility standards (accessible routes, restrooms, counters, entrances); when alteration work is $100,000+ that work must comply, and when work reaches 30% or more of the building's full and fair cash value the entire building must be brought into compliance.
chineseSummary: 521 CMR 由马萨诸塞州建筑无障碍委员会制定，是州建筑规范中针对向公众开放建筑（含餐厅）的无障碍专章，现行整套修订于2006年7月27日生效，由地方建筑检查员执行。改建工程造价达10万美元须使该部分合规；若工程达到建筑公平市值的30%及以上，则整栋建筑须整体合规。
englishSummary: 521 CMR sets Massachusetts-specific accessibility standards for buildings open to the public, including restaurants, and operates as a specialized part of the state building code enforced by local building inspectors. The current complete revision became effective July 27, 2006. Alteration work of $100,000 or more must comply, and work amounting to 30% or more of the building's full and fair cash value triggers full-building compliance.
businessImpact: A café build-out can unexpectedly trigger costly full-building accessibility upgrades once renovation spend crosses the 30%/$100k thresholds, so scope and budget must be planned early.
inspectionCitationRecord: AAB may issue fines of up to $1,000 per violation per day for willful noncompliance.
penalty: Up to $1,000 per violation per day for willful noncompliance; building permit/occupancy held until compliance or variance granted.
recommendedAction: Run a 521 CMR accessibility analysis at lease/design stage and confirm whether the build-out budget crosses the $100k or 30%-value triggers before committing to a site.

codeStandardName: 527 CMR 12.00 — Massachusetts Electrical Code (based on NFPA 70, 2026 National Electrical Code with MA amendments)
chineseTitle: 527 CMR 12.00 马萨诸塞州电气规范（基于2026版NFPA 70 国家电气规范并附州修订）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Electrical Code, 527 CMR 12.00 (NFPA 70, 2026)
agency: Board of Fire Prevention Regulations (BFPR) / Department of Fire Services; enforced by local wiring inspectors
codeCitation: 527 CMR 12.00 (adopting NFPA 70, 2026 Edition / National Electrical Code)
coveredEntities: All electrical installations in MA including commercial food-service and retail fit-outs / 马萨诸塞州所有电气安装工程，包括商业餐饮和零售装修
keyRequirements: All new and altered electrical installations must comply with the 2026 NEC as amended; the applicable code version vests at the electrical permit application date and work requires a licensed electrician, permit, and wiring inspection.
chineseSummary: 527 CMR 12.00 采用2026版NFPA 70（国家电气规范）并附州修订，于2026年4月24日生效，由地方电气检查员执行。适用版本以电气许可申请日为准。咖啡店设备（浓缩咖啡机、烤箱、HVAC等）的电气安装须由持证电工完成并通过验收。
englishSummary: 527 CMR 12.00 adopts the 2026 NFPA 70 (National Electrical Code) with Massachusetts amendments and is effective April 24, 2026, with the applicable version vesting at the electrical permit application date. A café's electrical work (espresso machines, ovens, HVAC, lighting) must be done by a licensed electrician under permit and pass wiring inspection.
businessImpact: Store electrical build-outs must meet the current NEC edition and pass wiring inspection before equipment energization and occupancy, affecting opening timelines.
penalty: Permit denial, failed wiring inspection, correction orders, and fines under M.G.L. c. 143/148; occupancy withheld until passing inspection.
recommendedAction: Use licensed MA electricians and pull electrical permits per store; confirm which NEC edition vests based on the electrical-permit application date.

codeStandardName: 248 CMR 10.00 — Uniform State Plumbing Code (Cross-Connection/Backflow §10.14 and Grease Interceptors §10.09)
chineseTitle: 248 CMR 10.00 统一州管道规范（防回流 §10.14 与隔油池 §10.09）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Uniform State Plumbing Code, 248 CMR 10.00
agency: Board of State Examiners of Plumbers and Gas Fitters; backflow approvals via MassDEP; enforced by local plumbing inspector
codeCitation: 248 CMR 10.00 (10.14 Water Supply/Cross-Connection; 10.09 Interceptors, Separators and Holding Tanks)
coveredEntities: All plumbing installations in MA food-preparation establishments / 马萨诸塞州所有食品制备场所的管道安装
keyRequirements: Potable water systems must be protected from cross-connection with approved backflow preventers (RPZ/double-check, with MassDEP approval), and fixtures producing grease-laden waste must discharge through code-sized grease interceptors.
chineseSummary: 248 CMR 10.00 统一州管道规范（2023年12月8日生效）由州管道与燃气检验委员会执行。§10.14 要求以经马萨诸塞州环保局批准的防回流装置（RPZ/双止回阀）保护饮用水系统免受交叉连接污染；§10.09 要求产生含油废水的设备经合规尺寸的隔油池排放。咖啡店的水槽、洗碗机等须据此安装。
englishSummary: The Uniform State Plumbing Code (248 CMR 10.00, effective December 8, 2023) requires potable-water systems to be protected from cross-connection using MassDEP-approved backflow preventers (§10.14) and requires grease-laden fixtures to discharge through properly sized grease interceptors (§10.09). A café's sinks, dishwashers, and beverage equipment must be plumbed accordingly under permit.
businessImpact: Backflow and grease-interceptor requirements drive plumbing design, MassDEP approvals, permit timing, and ongoing testing/cleaning costs at every food-service location.
penalty: No plumbing permit issued without required MassDEP backflow approval; correction orders and fines under M.G.L. c. 142; failed inspection delays occupancy.
recommendedAction: Engage a licensed MA plumber to design backflow protection (with MassDEP approval) and code-sized grease interception, and set up annual backflow testing and routine grease-trap cleaning.

codeStandardName: Federal OSHA Jurisdiction over Massachusetts Private-Sector Employers (Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970)
chineseTitle: 联邦OSHA对马萨诸塞州私营部门雇主的管辖（1970年《职业安全与健康法》）
englishTitle: Federal OSHA Coverage of Massachusetts Private-Sector Employers
agency: U.S. Department of Labor — Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), Boston South Area Office (Braintree, MA)
coveredEntities: All private-sector employers in Massachusetts, including cafés/restaurants / 马萨诸塞州所有私营部门雇主，包括咖啡店/餐厅
keyRequirements: Massachusetts has no state OSHA plan for private employers, so federal OSHA directly enforces the General Duty Clause and all 29 CFR 1910 standards against private cafés, including inspections and citations from the Braintree area office.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州没有覆盖私营部门的州OSHA计划，因此联邦OSHA直接对州内私营雇主（含咖啡店）执法，由位于布伦特里（Braintree）的波士顿南区办公室负责检查与开具罚单。马萨诸塞州的州计划仅覆盖州与地方政府雇员，不覆盖私营企业。这意味着瑞幸门店受联邦OSHA标准约束。
englishSummary: Massachusetts' OSHA state plan covers only state and local government workers, so private-sector employers like cafés remain under direct federal OSHA jurisdiction, enforced from the Boston South Area Office in Braintree. Federal OSHA applies the General Duty Clause and 29 CFR 1910 standards, conducts inspections, and issues citations to private MA workplaces.
businessImpact: MA store safety compliance is governed and inspected by federal OSHA (not a state plan), so the chain must follow federal 1910 standards and recordkeeping at every location.
inspectionCitationRecord: Federal OSHA Boston South (Braintree) Area Office conducts programmed and complaint-based inspections of MA private employers, including food-service establishments.
penalty: Federal OSHA penalties (e.g., up to ~$16,550 per serious violation; ~$165,514 for willful/repeat, adjusted annually).
recommendedAction: Treat MA stores as federal-OSHA-regulated workplaces: maintain 29 CFR 1910 compliance and 1904 injury/illness records, and know the Braintree area office is the responding authority.

codeStandardName: Massachusetts Smoke-Free Workplace Law, M.G.L. c. 270, § 22 (Posting/Signage and Smoking Prohibition)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州无烟工作场所法 M.G.L. c. 270 §22（禁烟及标识张贴）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Smoke-Free Workplace Law, M.G.L. c. 270, § 22
agency: Massachusetts Department of Public Health (DPH); enforced by local boards of health
coveredEntities: All enclosed workplaces in MA with one or more employees, including restaurants and cafés / 马萨诸塞州所有有一名及以上员工的封闭式工作场所，包括餐厅和咖啡店
keyRequirements: Smoking is prohibited in all enclosed workplaces (including cafés), employers must provide a smoke-free environment, and "No Smoking" signs must be conspicuously posted so they are clearly visible to employees, customers, and visitors.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州无烟工作场所法（M.G.L. c. 270 §22，2004年7月5日起施行）禁止在所有封闭式工作场所（含咖啡店）吸烟，雇主须提供无烟环境，并在显著位置张贴对员工、顾客和访客清晰可见的"禁止吸烟"标识。由州公共卫生部与地方卫生委员会执行。咖啡店须在入口及营业区张贴合规禁烟标识。
englishSummary: Since July 5, 2004, the Massachusetts Smoke-Free Workplace Law bans smoking in all enclosed workplaces, including cafés, and requires employers to keep the environment smoke-free. "No Smoking" signs must be conspicuously posted and clearly visible to employees and customers; the law is enforced by DPH and local boards of health.
businessImpact: Each café must post compliant no-smoking signage and prohibit indoor smoking/vaping or face fines from the local board of health.
inspectionCitationRecord: Local boards of health inspect and cite establishments that permit smoking or fail to post required signage.
penalty: Owner/manager fines of $100 (1st), $200 (2nd within 2 yrs), $300 (3rd+ within 2 yrs); repeated egregious violations may lead to license suspension/revocation.
recommendedAction: Post DPH-compliant "No Smoking" signage at entrances and in service areas of every MA store and enforce a no indoor smoking/vaping policy.

codeStandardName: 225 CMR 23.00 — Massachusetts Stretch Code and Municipal Opt-in Specialized Code (Commercial; 2025 amendments to IECC 2021 / ASHRAE 90.1-2019)
chineseTitle: 225 CMR 23.00 马萨诸塞州延伸节能规范及市政选用专项节能规范（商业；2025年对IECC2021/ASHRAE 90.1-2019的修订）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Stretch and Specialized Energy Code (Commercial), 225 CMR 23.00
agency: Department of Energy Resources (DOER); adopted/enforced via local building departments under M.G.L. c. 25A, § 6
codeCitation: 225 CMR 23.00 (2025 amendments to IECC 2021 and ASHRAE 90.1-2019)
coveredEntities: New commercial/multifamily construction and major alterations in municipalities that adopt the Stretch/Specialized Code / 已采纳延伸/专项规范的市镇中的新建商业/多户建筑及重大改建工程
keyRequirements: In adopting municipalities, new commercial construction and major renovations must meet the heightened energy-performance and (for the Specialized Code) electrification-ready / net-zero-aligned requirements of 225 CMR 23.00 rather than the base energy code.
chineseSummary: 225 CMR 23.00 是马萨诸塞州针对商业及多户建筑的延伸节能规范与市政选用专项节能规范，2025年修订版于2025年2月14日生效，由能源资源部依据 M.G.L. c. 25A §6 制定，在采纳该规范的市镇通过地方建筑部门执行。在波士顿、剑桥、牛顿等已采纳市镇，新建门店与重大改建须满足更高的节能性能及电气化/近零碳要求。
englishSummary: 225 CMR 23.00 contains the Stretch Code and the municipal opt-in Specialized (net-zero-aligned) Code for commercial and multifamily construction, with 2025 amendments effective February 14, 2025. In municipalities that have adopted these codes (e.g., Boston, Cambridge, Newton), new café build-outs and major renovations must meet stricter energy-performance and electrification-ready requirements than the base code.
businessImpact: Whether a city has adopted the Stretch/Specialized Code dictates HVAC, envelope, and electrification design for a new store, materially affecting build cost and equipment choices.
penalty: Permit denial and failed energy/plan review in adopting municipalities until the project meets 225 CMR 23.00; occupancy withheld.
recommendedAction: Confirm each target municipality's energy-code status (base vs. Stretch vs. Specialized) before site selection and design HVAC/envelope/electrical to the applicable 225 CMR 23.00 tier.

codeStandardName: Florida Building Code, Building, 8th Edition (2023)
chineseTitle: 佛罗里达州建筑规范（建筑卷）第8版（2023年）
englishTitle: Florida Building Code, Building, 8th Edition (2023)
agency: Florida Building Commission / Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR)
codeCitation: Fla. Admin. Code Ch. 61G20; FBC Building 8th Ed. (based on IBC 2021)
coveredEntities: 所有商业建筑的新建、改建与装修工程 (All new construction, alteration and tenant build-out of commercial buildings statewide)
keyRequirements: All commercial construction, alterations and tenant build-outs (including coffee-shop fit-outs) permitted on or after Dec 31, 2023 must comply with the 8th Edition FBC, which is based on the 2021 International Building Code with Florida-specific amendments.
chineseSummary: 佛罗里达州建筑规范第8版（基于2021年国际建筑规范并含佛州专属修订）于2023年12月31日生效，州内统一适用，地方政府负责许可与执法。所有在该日期及之后申请许可的商业新建、改建及租户装修工程均须遵守此版本。咖啡店选址装修必须按此规范申报图纸并通过检查。
englishSummary: The 8th Edition (2023) Florida Building Code, based on IBC 2021 with Florida amendments, took effect statewide on December 31, 2023, with permitting and enforcement by local building departments. Any commercial new construction, alteration or tenant build-out applying for a permit on or after that date must comply with this edition. Café build-outs must submit plans and pass inspections under this code.
businessImpact: Every new Luckin Coffee location built or renovated in Florida must be permitted and inspected against the 8th Edition FBC before it can legally open.
penalty: Local code-enforcement fines (commonly $100-$500/day per violation), stop-work orders, and denial/revocation of permits or certificate of occupancy.
recommendedAction: Engage a Florida-licensed architect/contractor to design and permit each store build-out under the 8th Edition FBC and retain approved plan sets and inspection records.

codeStandardName: Florida Building Code, Accessibility, 8th Edition (2023)
chineseTitle: 佛罗里达州建筑规范（无障碍卷）第8版（2023年）
englishTitle: Florida Building Code, Accessibility, 8th Edition (2023)
agency: Florida Building Commission / DBPR
codeCitation: § 553.503, Fla. Stat.; FBC Accessibility 8th Ed. (incorporates 2010 ADA Standards)
coveredEntities: 公共accommodation场所与商业设施的设计、建造与改建 (Design, construction and alteration of places of public accommodation and commercial facilities)
keyRequirements: Florida's state accessibility code, adopted under §553.503 Fla. Stat. and incorporating the federal 2010 ADA Standards, mandates accessible routes, entrances, restrooms, counters and parking in all newly built or altered places of public accommodation, enforced through the local building-permit process.
chineseSummary: 佛州无障碍规范依据《佛州法规》第553.503节制定，纳入联邦《2010年ADA无障碍设计标准》，并通过地方建筑许可流程强制执行，与FBC同步于2023年12月31日生效。规范要求公共accommodation场所提供无障碍通道、入口、卫生间、服务柜台与停车位。这使ADA无障碍要求在州层面成为可在施工许可阶段强制执行的硬性条款。
englishSummary: Florida's Accessibility Code, adopted under §553.503 Fla. Stat. and incorporating the federal 2010 ADA Standards, took effect with the FBC on December 31, 2023 and is enforced at the local building-permit stage. It requires accessible routes, entrances, restrooms, service counters and parking in newly built or altered public accommodations. This makes ADA accessibility a hard, plan-reviewable requirement at permitting rather than only a private-litigation risk.
businessImpact: A café's counters, restrooms, entry, and path of travel must meet Florida accessibility requirements to pass plan review and inspection, and noncompliance also exposes the chain to federal ADA Title III lawsuits.
penalty: Permit/CO denial at the local level; separately, federal ADA Title III civil penalties (statutory $75,000 first / $150,000 subsequent, but inflation-adjusted by DOJ — $115,231 / $230,464 as of 2024 and rising annually) plus injunctive relief and plaintiff attorney fees.
recommendedAction: Have the design team certify each store layout against the Florida Accessibility Code / 2010 ADA Standards before permit submittal and re-check after any tenant alterations.

codeStandardName: Florida Fire Prevention Code, 8th Edition (2023) — NFPA 1 & NFPA 101
chineseTitle: 佛罗里达州消防规范第8版（2023年）——NFPA 1与NFPA 101
englishTitle: Florida Fire Prevention Code, 8th Edition (2023) (NFPA 1 Fire Code & NFPA 101 Life Safety Code)
agency: Florida State Fire Marshal, Department of Financial Services
codeCitation: Fla. Admin. Code Ch. 69A-60; NFPA 1 (2021, FL) & NFPA 101 (2021, FL)
coveredEntities: 所有商业占用场所，含餐饮/集会用途的运营与改建 (All commercial occupancies, including mercantile/assembly food-service uses)
keyRequirements: The 8th Edition Florida Fire Prevention Code (Florida-amended NFPA 1 and NFPA 101) governs means of egress, occupant load, fire extinguishers, alarms, and commercial cooking fire protection, and is enforced through fire-marshal plan review and annual operating inspections.
chineseSummary: 佛州消防规范第8版采用2021年版NFPA 1（消防规范）和NFPA 101（生命安全规范）的佛州修订版，于2023年12月31日生效。规范涵盖疏散通道、占用人数、灭火器、火警系统与商用烹饪防火等，由州消防局及地方消防部门通过图纸审查与年度运营检查执法。咖啡店开业前及每年均须接受消防检查。
englishSummary: The 8th Edition Florida Fire Prevention Code adopts the Florida-amended 2021 NFPA 1 Fire Code and NFPA 101 Life Safety Code and took effect December 31, 2023. It governs egress, occupant load, extinguishers, alarms and commercial cooking fire protection, enforced via fire-marshal plan review and annual operating inspections. Cafés must pass fire inspection before opening and on a recurring basis.
businessImpact: A Luckin store cannot obtain or keep its operating approval without passing fire-marshal plan review and recurring inspections covering exits, occupant load, and extinguishers.
penalty: Fire-marshal correction orders, fines, and authority to order the premises closed/evacuated until life-safety violations are corrected.
recommendedAction: Schedule fire-marshal plan review during build-out and maintain a tickler for annual fire inspections, extinguisher service, and alarm testing at every Florida location.

codeStandardName: Cross-Connection Control for Public Water Systems
chineseTitle: 公共供水系统交叉连接控制（防回流）规则
englishTitle: Cross-Connection Control for Public Water Systems
agency: Florida Department of Environmental Protection (FDEP)
coveredEntities: 连接公共供水系统、存在交叉连接风险的客户设施（含商用餐饮场所）(Customer premises connected to a public water system with cross-connection hazards, including commercial food-service)
keyRequirements: Rule 62-555.360 requires community water systems to run a cross-connection control program (per AWWA Manual M14) that compels hazard premises to install and annually test backflow-prevention assemblies, prohibiting cross-connections without approved backflow protection.
chineseSummary: 佛州行政法规62-555.360节要求社区供水系统建立交叉连接控制计划（参照AWWA M14手册），对有污染风险的客户设施强制安装防回流装置并每年测试。咖啡机、制冰机、清洗槽等设备通常被认定为污染风险，需配备经认证的防回流组件并由持证人员年检。无防回流保护的交叉连接被禁止。
englishSummary: Florida Administrative Code R. 62-555.360 (current text effective May 5, 2014) requires each community water system to operate a cross-connection control program (following AWWA Manual M14) that forces hazard premises to install and annually test backflow-prevention assemblies and prohibits unprotected cross-connections. Coffee equipment, ice machines and mop/prep sinks are typically deemed hazards requiring certified backflow assemblies tested annually by a qualified tester.
businessImpact: Each Florida café likely must install a certified backflow preventer and pass an annual test or risk water-service shutoff by the local utility.
penalty: Local water utility may discontinue water service for noncompliance; FDEP enforcement against the water system can flow down to noncompliant customers.
recommendedAction: Confirm backflow-prevention requirements with the local water utility for each site and schedule annual certified backflow testing.

codeStandardName: HB 433 — Employment Regulations (preemption of local heat-exposure and employment ordinances)
chineseTitle: 众议院第433号法案——就业监管（先占地方高温暴露及用工条例）
englishTitle: HB 433 (2024) — Employment Regulations (preemption of local heat-exposure requirements)
agency: Florida Legislature (signed by the Governor)
codeCitation: Ch. 2024-209, Laws of Fla. (CS/CS/HB 433); §§ 218.077, 448.095 et al., Fla. Stat.
coveredEntities: 佛州境内所有雇主，包括连锁咖啡店及其承包商 (All employers in Florida, including chain cafés and their contractors)
keyRequirements: Effective July 1, 2024, HB 433 bars Florida cities and counties from mandating workplace heat-exposure protections (e.g., water/shade/rest breaks) beyond state or federal law, voiding any conflicting local ordinance and leaving federal OSHA's General Duty Clause as the operative heat standard.
chineseSummary: 佛州众议院第433号法案于2024年7月1日生效，禁止市县政府要求雇主提供超出州或联邦法律的工作场所高温保护（如饮水、遮阴、休息），相冲突的地方条例一律无效。这意味着佛州不存在地方强制高温规则，雇主对高温的法律义务回归联邦OSHA的'一般责任条款'。雇主仍须在联邦OSHA框架下防范可识别的高温危害。
englishSummary: Florida's HB 433, effective July 1, 2024, prohibits municipalities and counties from requiring employer heat-exposure protections (water, shade, rest breaks) beyond state or federal law and voids conflicting local ordinances. The result is no local mandatory heat rule in Florida; the operative obligation reverts to federal OSHA's General Duty Clause. Employers must still address recognized heat hazards under federal OSHA.
businessImpact: Luckin need not follow a patchwork of local Florida heat ordinances, but its kitchens/back-of-house remain exposed to federal OSHA General Duty Clause enforcement for excessive heat.
inspectionCitationRecord: Federal OSHA has cited employers for heat hazards under the General Duty Clause (Section 5(a)(1)) nationwide; Florida workplaces fall under federal OSHA jurisdiction.
recommendedAction: Drop reliance on any preempted local heat ordinance and standardize a company heat-illness prevention program that satisfies federal OSHA General Duty expectations.

codeStandardName: Florida Building Code, 9th Edition (2026) — code update in development
chineseTitle: 佛罗里达州建筑规范第9版（2026年）——更新制定中
englishTitle: Florida Building Code, 9th Edition (2026) (in development)
agency: Florida Building Commission / DBPR
codeCitation: FBC 9th Ed. (based on 2024 I-Codes & ASCE 7-22); §553.73, Fla. Stat. (triennial update)
coveredEntities: 未来在该规范生效后申请许可的所有商业建造与改建 (All commercial construction/alteration permitted after the edition's effective date)
keyRequirements: The 9th Edition FBC, based on the 2024 International Codes and ASCE 7-22 and posted for review in December 2025, is expected to take effect on or about December 31, 2026, updating structural, energy, accessibility and other requirements for future permits.
chineseSummary: 佛州建筑委员会正按三年周期制定第9版建筑规范，以2024年版国际规范及ASCE 7-22结构标准为基础，已于2025年12月22日上网公示。预计在2026年12月31日前后生效，届时将取代第8版并更新结构、节能、无障碍等要求。届时申请许可的新店建造须采用新版本。
englishSummary: The Florida Building Commission is developing the 9th Edition FBC on the triennial cycle, based on the 2024 I-Codes and ASCE 7-22, posted online for review on December 22, 2025. It is expected to take effect around December 31, 2026, replacing the 8th Edition and updating structural, energy and accessibility provisions. New store permits filed after that date will follow the new edition.
businessImpact: Store-development standards (energy, structural, accessibility details) will shift for any Florida build-out permitted from late 2026 onward, affecting design budgets and timelines.
recommendedAction: Track the 9th Edition adoption schedule and brief the design/construction team so projects permitted after the effective date use the correct code edition.

codeStandardName: 2017 District of Columbia Building Code (DCMR Title 12-A)
chineseTitle: 2017年华盛顿特区建筑规范（DCMR第12-A编）
englishTitle: 2017 District of Columbia Building Code (DCMR Title 12-A)
agency: DC Department of Buildings (DOB)
codeCitation: DCMR Title 12-A (2015 IBC as amended by 2017 DC Construction Codes Supplement)
coveredEntities: 特区内所有商业建筑的建造、改建与装修 (All commercial construction, alteration and build-out in the District)
keyRequirements: The 2017 DC Building Code (2015 IBC plus DC amendments) became effective May 29, 2020 and governs structural, occupancy, egress and build-out requirements for all commercial construction and alterations in the District, enforced by DOB permit and inspection.
chineseSummary: 2017年特区建筑规范以2015年国际建筑规范加特区修订为基础，于2020年5月29日生效，由特区建筑局（DOB）通过许可与检查执法。规范涵盖结构、占用分类、疏散及租户装修等要求，适用于特区内所有商业建造与改建。咖啡店在特区开设新店须据此申报并通过检查。
englishSummary: The 2017 DC Building Code (2015 IBC as amended by the DC Construction Codes Supplement) took effect May 29, 2020 and governs structural, occupancy, egress and build-out requirements for all commercial construction and alterations, enforced through DOB permits and inspections. It is the current adopted building code for the District. A café opening in DC must permit and inspect its fit-out under this code.
businessImpact: Any DC store build-out must be designed, permitted and inspected under the 2017 DC Building Code before it can open.
penalty: DOB stop-work orders, permit denial, and fines under the DC Construction Codes for unpermitted or noncompliant work.
recommendedAction: Use a DC-licensed design professional to permit each build-out under DCMR 12-A and retain stamped plans and inspection sign-offs.

codeStandardName: 2017 District of Columbia Fire Code (DCMR Title 12-H)
chineseTitle: 2017年华盛顿特区消防规范（DCMR第12-H编）
englishTitle: 2017 District of Columbia Fire Code (DCMR Title 12-H)
agency: DC Fire and Emergency Medical Services (FEMS) — Fire Marshal; DC DOB
codeCitation: DCMR Title 12-H (2015 IFC as amended by 2017 DC Construction Codes Supplement)
coveredEntities: 特区内所有商业占用场所，含含商用烹饪的餐饮场所 (All commercial occupancies in the District, including food-service with commercial cooking)
keyRequirements: The 2017 DC Fire Code (2015 IFC plus DC amendments), effective May 29, 2020, sets means-of-egress, fire-protection, extinguisher and commercial-cooking (Type I hood / suppression per IFC §607 & NFPA 96) requirements enforced by the DC Fire Marshal and DOB.
chineseSummary: 2017年特区消防规范以2015年国际消防规范加特区修订为基础，于2020年5月29日生效，规定疏散、消防保护、灭火器及商用烹饪（按IFC第607节与NFPA 96配置I型排烟罩及灭火系统）等要求。由特区消防局火警官与建筑局执法。设有热厨/烹饪设备的咖啡店须满足排烟罩、灭火及定期清洗检查要求。
englishSummary: The 2017 DC Fire Code (2015 IFC as amended by the DC Construction Codes Supplement) took effect May 29, 2020 and sets egress, fire-protection, extinguisher and commercial-cooking requirements (Type I hood and suppression per IFC §607 and NFPA 96), enforced by the DC Fire Marshal and DOB. Food-service occupancies with grease-producing cooking must meet hood, suppression and recurring cleaning/inspection requirements.
businessImpact: DC cafés with hot-food/grease cooking must install and maintain hood suppression and pass fire inspections to operate, and all stores need compliant exits and extinguishers.
penalty: Fire Marshal correction orders, fines, and authority to suspend occupancy for life-safety/fire violations.
recommendedAction: Confirm whether each DC location triggers Type I hood/suppression rules and maintain a schedule for hood cleaning, suppression service, and annual fire inspections.

codeStandardName: Certificate of Occupancy required before use (DC Code § 6-641.09)
chineseTitle: 使用前须取得占用许可证（DC法典第6-641.09节）
englishTitle: Certificate of Occupancy Required Before Use (D.C. Code § 6-641.09)
agency: DC Department of Buildings (DOB)
coveredEntities: 特区内除独栋住宅外的所有建筑使用，含所有商业用途 (All building use in the District other than single-family dwellings, including all commercial uses)
keyRequirements: Under D.C. Code § 6-641.09 it is unlawful to use any building or land until a Certificate of Occupancy is obtained, and a C of O verifies compliance with Zoning Regulations, the DC Construction Codes and the Green Building Act for the specific use and occupant load.
chineseSummary: 根据《特区法典》第6-641.09节，未取得占用许可证（C of O）前使用任何建筑或土地均属违法；除独栋住宅外的所有商业用途均需办理。占用许可证确认特定用途及占用人数符合分区条例（DCMR第11编）、特区建筑规范及绿色建筑法。每家咖啡店开业及变更用途时都必须取得对应的占用许可证。
englishSummary: Under D.C. Code § 6-641.09 it is unlawful to use any building, structure or land until a Certificate of Occupancy is first obtained; this applies to all commercial uses. The C of O certifies that the specific use and occupant load comply with the Zoning Regulations (DCMR Title 11), the DC Construction Codes and the Green Building Act. A new café (or a change of use/occupant load) requires a corresponding C of O.
businessImpact: A Luckin store in DC cannot legally open its doors to the public until DOB issues a Certificate of Occupancy for the café use and occupant load.
penalty: Fines up to $100 per day for each day a violation continues, plus civil fines and injunctive action by the Attorney General.
recommendedAction: Sequence each DC opening so the Certificate of Occupancy (matching the café use and approved occupant load) is issued before any public operation begins.

codeStandardName: 2010 ADA Standards for Accessible Design — Title III Public Accommodations (federal, applicable in DC)
chineseTitle: 《2010年ADA无障碍设计标准》——第三章公共accommodation（联邦，适用于特区）
englishTitle: 2010 ADA Standards for Accessible Design — Title III Public Accommodations (federal)
agency: U.S. Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division
codeCitation: 28 CFR Part 36, Subpart D; 2010 ADA Standards (36 CFR Part 1191 app. B & D)
coveredEntities: 公共accommodation场所与商业设施（含面向公众的咖啡店）(Places of public accommodation and commercial facilities, including public-serving cafés)
keyRequirements: Compliance with the 2010 ADA Standards has been mandatory for new construction and alterations of public accommodations since March 15, 2012, requiring accessible entrances, routes, counters (including a portion of service counters), and restrooms, with readily achievable barrier removal in existing facilities.
chineseSummary: 《2010年ADA无障碍设计标准》自2012年3月15日起对公共accommodation的新建与改建强制适用，由美国司法部依据《美国残疾人法》第三章执行。要求无障碍入口、通道、服务柜台（含部分低位柜台）及卫生间，既有设施则须进行'可合理实现'的障碍清除。该联邦标准在特区直接适用，与地方建筑规范并行。咖啡店属公共accommodation，须全面合规。处罚金额每年随通胀调整（2014年法定为7.5万/15万美元，2024年已升至约11.5万/23万美元）。
englishSummary: The 2010 ADA Standards have been mandatory for new construction and alterations of public accommodations since March 15, 2012, enforced by DOJ under ADA Title III. They require accessible entrances, routes, service counters (including a lowered portion) and restrooms, and readily achievable barrier removal in existing facilities. This federal standard applies directly in DC alongside local building codes; cafés are public accommodations and must comply.
businessImpact: As public accommodations, all Luckin cafés (existing and new) face federal ADA Title III exposure for inaccessible entrances, counters, or restrooms regardless of local permit sign-off.
inspectionCitationRecord: DOJ has entered numerous ADA Title III settlements/consent decrees against retail and food-service chains for inaccessible facilities; private plaintiffs routinely file Title III suits over service counters and entrances.
penalty: DOJ civil penalties (statutory $75,000 first / $150,000 subsequent, inflation-adjusted by DOJ to $115,231 / $230,464 as of 2024 and rising annually), plus injunctive relief; private suits recover injunctive relief and attorney fees.
recommendedAction: Audit every DC store against the 2010 ADA Standards (entrance, path of travel, accessible counter segment, restroom) and remediate readily achievable barriers, in addition to meeting local code.

codeStandardName: Occupational Noise Exposure (Hearing Conservation)
chineseTitle: 职业噪声暴露标准（听力保护）
englishTitle: OSHA Occupational Noise Exposure Standard — Hearing Conservation
agency: U.S. Department of Labor — Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)
coveredEntities: 所有受OSHA管辖的一般行业雇主（员工噪声暴露达到8小时时间加权平均85分贝及以上者）/ All OSHA-covered general-industry employers whose employees are exposed at or above an 8-hour TWA of 85 dBA
keyRequirements: Employers must implement a hearing conservation program (noise monitoring, audiometric testing, hearing protectors at no cost, and training) when employee noise reaches an 8-hour TWA of 85 dBA, with feasible engineering/administrative controls required above the 90 dBA permissible exposure limit.
chineseSummary: 该标准最初于1971年颁布，1983年增设听力保护修正案。允许暴露限值（PEL）为8小时时间加权平均90分贝；当达到85分贝的“行动水平”时，雇主须实施持续有效的听力保护计划，包括噪声监测、年度听力测试、免费提供护听器及培训。
englishSummary: First promulgated in 1971 and amended in 1983 to add the Hearing Conservation Amendment, the standard sets a 90 dBA 8-hour TWA permissible exposure limit and an 85 dBA action level. At or above the action level, employers must run a continuing hearing conservation program covering monitoring, annual audiometric testing, no-cost hearing protectors, and training.
businessImpact: High-output espresso grinders, blenders, ice machines and back-of-house equipment can push baristas toward the 85 dBA action level, potentially obligating noise monitoring and a hearing-conservation program.
inspectionCitationRecord: OSHA routinely cites 29 CFR 1910.95(c) (failure to administer a hearing conservation program) and 1910.95(d) (failure to monitor) in general-industry inspections.
penalty: OSHA serious violations up to ~$16,550 per violation; willful/repeat up to ~$165,514 per violation (2024 maximums, annually adjusted)
recommendedAction: Conduct a baseline noise survey in busy stores; if exposures approach 85 dBA TWA, implement monitoring, offer audiometric testing and hearing protection, and train staff.

codeStandardName: Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act of 1986 (Proposition 65)
chineseTitle: 1986年安全饮用水和毒性物质执法法案（第65号提案）
englishTitle: California Proposition 65 — Clear and Reasonable Warnings
agency: California Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment (OEHHA); enforced by the CA Attorney General
codeCitation: Cal. Health & Safety Code §§ 25249.6–25249.7 (10-employee threshold at § 25249.11(b)); 27 CCR §§ 25600 et seq.
coveredEntities: 在加州经营且雇有10名或以上员工的企业 / Businesses operating in California with 10 or more employees
keyRequirements: Businesses with 10+ employees must provide a 'clear and reasonable' warning before knowingly exposing anyone to a listed cancer-causing or reproductive-toxicant chemical, e.g. via labeling, point-of-display, or premises signage, unless the exposure is below the applicable safe-harbor level.
chineseSummary: 第65号提案要求拥有10名及以上员工的企业，在使人接触加州列明的致癌或生殖毒性化学物质前，提供“清晰合理”的警告。注意：OEHHA于2019年通过27 CCR §25704明确规定，咖啡烘焙/冲泡过程中产生的化学物质（含丙烯酰胺）不构成显著风险，咖啡无需张贴第65号提案致癌警告。其他受管制化学物质（如清洁剂、装修材料）的暴露仍可能触发警告义务。违规可处每日每项最高2,500美元罚款。
englishSummary: Proposition 65 requires businesses with 10 or more employees to warn before exposing people to chemicals the state lists as causing cancer or reproductive harm. Important nuance: OEHHA's 2019 regulation (27 CCR § 25704) established that chemicals (including acrylamide) created by roasting/brewing coffee pose no significant risk, so coffee itself does NOT require a Prop 65 cancer warning. Warning obligations may still arise from other listed-chemical exposures at a café (e.g., cleaning agents, build-out materials). Civil penalties reach $2,500 per day per violation.
businessImpact: Although the well-known coffee/acrylamide warning is no longer required under 27 CCR § 25704 (2019), a California café can still face Prop 65 exposure from other listed chemicals (e.g., cleaning products, furnishings, build-out materials) and is a frequent private-enforcer ('bounty hunter') litigation target.
inspectionCitationRecord: Coffee retailers were historically targeted by Prop 65 acrylamide enforcement, most prominently in CERT v. Starbucks et al. (Los Angeles Superior Court, BC435759, filed 2010); that coffee theory was effectively mooted by OEHHA's 2019 coffee regulation (27 CCR § 25704).
penalty: Civil penalties up to $2,500 per day per violation (Health & Safety Code § 25249.7), plus plaintiff attorney fees
recommendedAction: Do not rely on (or post) a coffee/acrylamide warning, which 27 CCR § 25704 has rendered unnecessary; instead inventory other listed-chemical exposures (cleaning agents, build-out materials) and post OEHHA-compliant warnings only where a genuine above-safe-harbor exposure exists.

codeStandardName: Local Law 152 of 2016 — Periodic Inspection of Gas Piping Systems
chineseTitle: 2016年第152号地方法——燃气管道系统定期检查
englishTitle: NYC Local Law 152 of 2016 — Periodic Gas Piping System Inspections
agency: New York City Department of Buildings (DOB)
codeCitation: NYC Administrative Code § 28-318.3 (added by Local Law 152/2016)
coveredEntities: 除一、二户住宅（R-3）外，几乎所有设有燃气管道的纽约市建筑的业主 / Owners of nearly all NYC buildings with gas piping, except most one- and two-family (R-3) homes
keyRequirements: Building owners must have exposed gas piping inspected by a Licensed Master Plumber at least once every four years (on a rolling community-district schedule) and file a certification (GPS2) with the DOB within 60 days.
chineseSummary: 2016年第152号地方法在重大燃气爆炸事故后出台，要求几乎所有设有燃气管道的纽约市建筑的业主每四年由持牌总管道工（LMP）检查一次外露燃气管道。LMP须在检查后30天内出具GPS1报告，业主须在60天内通过DOB门户提交GPS2认证。检查按社区学区滚动周期进行。
englishSummary: Enacted after fatal NYC gas explosions, Local Law 152 requires owners of nearly all buildings with gas piping to have exposed gas piping inspected by a Licensed Master Plumber at least once every four years on a rolling community-district cycle. The LMP issues a GPS1 report within 30 days and the owner must file a GPS2 certification with DOB within 60 days.
businessImpact: A café occupying or leasing space in a NYC building with gas-fired equipment must ensure the building's gas piping is inspected and certified on the four-year cycle, or its gas service and operations can be jeopardized.
inspectionCitationRecord: DOB issues 'Notice of Gas Piping System Certification' failures and Class 2 violations to owners who miss the LL152 GPS2 filing deadline.
penalty: DOB civil penalties for failure to file (e.g., $5,000) plus potential re-inspection and gas-service consequences
recommendedAction: Confirm the building's community-district inspection year and verify the landlord/owner has a current LL152 GPS2 certification on file with DOB before relying on gas service.

codeStandardName: Florida Fire Prevention Code (NFPA 1 / IFC §5307.3) — Insulated Liquid Carbon Dioxide Beverage-Dispensing Systems
chineseTitle: 佛州消防预防规范——饮料分配用绝缘液态二氧化碳系统
englishTitle: Insulated Liquid Carbon Dioxide Systems Used in Beverage Dispensing (IFC/NFPA §5307.3)
agency: Florida State Fire Marshal (Dept. of Financial Services); local fire code officials; standard from the International Fire Code / NFPA
codeCitation: International Fire Code §5307.3 (Florida Fire Prevention Code, 8th Ed., NFPA 1); also CA Fire Code §5307
coveredEntities: 使用超过100磅二氧化碳的室内饮料分配系统（如汽水机、咖啡碳酸饮料系统）的场所 / Facilities with indoor beverage-dispensing CO2 systems exceeding 100 lbs (e.g., soda/carbonated-beverage and coffee systems)
keyRequirements: Indoor insulated liquid CO2 beverage systems with more than 100 lbs of CO2 must provide mechanical ventilation or a compliant gas-detection system (alarms at 5,000 ppm and 30,000 ppm) plus hazard signage at storage and dispensing areas.
chineseSummary: 国际消防规范（IFC）第5307.3条（已被佛州第8版消防规范及加州消防规范采纳）规定，室内超过100磅二氧化碳的饮料分配系统须配备机械通风或合规的气体检测系统（在5,000 ppm发出监控警报、30,000 ppm发出现场警报），并在储存和分配区域张贴危险标识。该条旨在防止二氧化碳泄漏导致窒息。
englishSummary: IFC Section 5307.3 (adopted in the Florida Fire Prevention Code and the California Fire Code) governs indoor insulated liquid CO2 beverage-dispensing systems exceeding 100 lbs of CO2. Such systems must have mechanical ventilation or a gas-detection system that alarms at 5,000 ppm (supervisory) and 30,000 ppm (local), plus hazard signage, to prevent asphyxiation from CO2 leaks.
businessImpact: Cafés serving carbonated/cold-brew nitro or soda drinks often use bulk insulated CO2 tanks; exceeding 100 lbs indoors triggers mandatory CO2 detection alarms and signage subject to fire-marshal inspection.
inspectionCitationRecord: Fire marshals routinely flag missing CO2 gas-detection/alarm systems and signage in restaurants and beverage venues with bulk CO2 storage during annual fire inspections.
penalty: Local fire-code violations; failure to correct can result in fines and suspension of operation pending compliance
recommendedAction: Inventory CO2 tank capacity per store; for indoor systems over 100 lbs, install a code-compliant CO2 gas-detection/alarm system and post required hazard signage before the fire inspection.

codeStandardName: Florida Clean Indoor Air Act — Posting of Signs and No-Smoking/Vaping Policy
chineseTitle: 佛罗里达清洁室内空气法——禁烟/禁电子烟标识与政策
englishTitle: Florida Clean Indoor Air Act — No-Smoking/Vaping Signage and Policy
agency: Florida Department of Health; Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR)
coveredEntities: 佛州境内的封闭式室内工作场所（包括咖啡馆等公共场所）的负责人 / Proprietors/persons in charge of enclosed indoor workplaces in Florida, including cafés
keyRequirements: Smoking and vaping are prohibited in enclosed indoor workplaces, and the proprietor must develop and implement a written no-smoking/vaping policy (signage is permitted at the proprietor's discretion).
chineseSummary: 佛罗里达清洁室内空气法（佛州法典第386章第二部分）禁止在封闭式室内工作场所吸烟和使用电子烟。场所负责人须制定并实施书面的禁烟/禁电子烟政策；张贴标识由负责人酌情决定但属常见合规做法。该法由佛州卫生部和DBPR执行。
englishSummary: The Florida Clean Indoor Air Act (Ch. 386, Part II) prohibits smoking and vaping in enclosed indoor workplaces. The person in charge must develop and implement a written no-smoking/vaping policy; posting signs is at the proprietor's discretion but is standard compliance practice. The Department of Health and DBPR enforce the law.
businessImpact: Every Florida café is an enclosed indoor workplace, so management must maintain a written no-smoking/vaping policy and typically post signage to avoid Department of Health enforcement.
inspectionCitationRecord: Florida DOH/DBPR investigate complaints under Ch. 386 and can issue notices of violation to enclosed workplaces lacking a compliant policy.
penalty: Administrative penalties under §386.208: first violation up to $250; subsequent violations up to $500 per incident
recommendedAction: Adopt and document a written no-smoking/vaping policy for Florida stores and post clear no-smoking/no-vaping signage at entrances.

codeStandardName: 2017 District of Columbia Construction Codes (incl. Accessibility, A117.1) — DCMR Title 12
chineseTitle: 2017年华盛顿特区建筑规范（含无障碍规范A117.1）——DCMR第12编
englishTitle: 2017 DC Construction Codes (including Accessibility Code, ICC A117.1) — DCMR Title 12
agency: District of Columbia Department of Buildings (DOB)
codeCitation: DCMR Title 12 (Sections A–M); Accessibility based on ICC A117.1 (2017)
coveredEntities: 在华盛顿特区进行新建、改建或用途变更的所有建筑业主与承租人 / All building owners and tenants undertaking construction, alteration, or change of use in the District of Columbia
keyRequirements: All construction, alterations, and changes of use in DC must comply with the 2017 DC Construction Codes (2015 I-Codes plus DC amendments), including building, mechanical, plumbing, fire, energy, and accessibility (ICC A117.1) provisions.
chineseSummary: 2017年华盛顿特区建筑规范（编入DCMR第12编A至M节）以2015年ICC系列示范规范、2014年国家电气规范和2013年ASHRAE 90.1为基础并经特区修订，于2020年5月29日生效。规范涵盖建筑、机械、管道、消防、节能和以ICC A117.1为基础的无障碍要求，适用于特区内的新建、改建和用途变更工程。
englishSummary: The 2017 DC Construction Codes, codified at DCMR Title 12 (Sections A–M), are based on the 2015 ICC model codes, 2014 NEC, and 2013 ASHRAE 90.1 as amended by the District, and took effect May 29, 2020. They include building, mechanical, plumbing, fire, energy, and accessibility (ICC A117.1) requirements governing all construction, alterations, and changes of use in DC.
businessImpact: Building out or altering a DC café triggers the 2017 Construction Codes — driving permit-drawing requirements for layout, restrooms, mechanical/plumbing, and ADA/A117.1 accessibility that gate the certificate of occupancy.
inspectionCitationRecord: DC DOB issues stop-work orders and code-violation notices for construction performed without permits or not conforming to the Title 12 codes.
penalty: DC DOB civil infractions and fines for code/permit violations; stop-work orders until corrected
recommendedAction: Engage a DC-licensed design professional to ensure café build-out drawings meet the 2017 DC Construction Codes (including A117.1 accessibility) before filing for permits.

codeStandardName: Florida Building Code, Building, 9th Edition (2026)
chineseTitle: 佛罗里达建筑规范（建筑卷）第9版（2026）
englishTitle: Florida Building Code, Building, 9th Edition (2026)
agency: Florida Building Commission (Department of Business and Professional Regulation)
codeCitation: Florida Building Code, 9th Edition (based on 2024 I-Codes), Rule 61G20, F.A.C.
coveredEntities: 佛州境内未来进行新建、改建及用途变更的所有建筑业主、设计方与承包商 / All owners, designers, and contractors undertaking future construction, alteration, or change of use in Florida
keyRequirements: Once effective, the 9th Edition (developed from the 2024 I-Codes with Florida amendments) will replace the 8th Edition as the mandatory statewide construction standard for new construction and alterations.
chineseSummary: 佛罗里达建筑委员会自2024年起依据2024年ICC示范规范开发佛州建筑规范第9版。各技术咨询委员会审查了逾1,500项I-Code修改建议，草案已于2025年12月在线公布供审查。预计在公众评议和委员会表决后约于2026年底（2026年12月31日）生效，届时将取代现行第8版（2023）。
englishSummary: The Florida Building Commission has been developing the 9th Edition of the Florida Building Code based on the 2024 ICC model codes since 2024. Technical Advisory Committees reviewed over 1,500 proposed I-Code changes, the draft was posted online for review in December 2025, and the code is scheduled to take effect December 31, 2026 (per the statutory six-month publication buffer), replacing the current 8th Edition (2023).
businessImpact: New Florida store build-outs permitted after the 9th Edition's effective date must meet the updated structural, energy, and accessibility provisions, affecting design specs and construction budgets for the expansion pipeline.
recommendedAction: Track the Florida Building Commission's 9th Edition adoption calendar and align Florida store-design standards to the new code before its December 31, 2026 effective date.

codeStandardName: Compressed Gases (General Requirements)
chineseTitle: 压缩气体（一般要求）
englishTitle: OSHA Compressed Gases — General Requirements
agency: U.S. Department of Labor — Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)
coveredEntities: 在场所内储存、搬运或使用压缩气体钢瓶（如二氧化碳、氮气）的一般行业雇主 / General-industry employers that store, handle, or use compressed-gas cylinders (e.g., CO2, nitrogen) on premises
keyRequirements: Employers must visually verify compressed-gas cylinders are in safe condition and handle, store, and use them in accordance with Compressed Gas Association Pamphlet P-1 (e.g., securing cylinders upright and protecting valves).
chineseSummary: OSHA第1910.101条规定，雇主须通过目视检查确认压缩气体钢瓶处于安全状态，并按压缩气体协会P-1指南进行厂内搬运、储存和使用。咖啡馆常用的二氧化碳和氮气钢瓶须妥善固定、直立放置并保护阀门，防止倾倒和泄漏。
englishSummary: OSHA's compressed-gas standard requires employers to ensure cylinders are safe by visual inspection and to handle, store, and use them per Compressed Gas Association Pamphlet P-1. For cafés, this means CO2 and nitrogen cylinders must be secured upright with valve protection to prevent tip-over and leaks.
businessImpact: Baristas handle CO2/nitrogen cylinders for carbonated and nitro beverages, so stores must secure and inspect cylinders to avoid OSHA citations and tip-over/asphyxiation hazards.
inspectionCitationRecord: OSHA cites 29 CFR 1910.101(b) for improperly stored or unsecured compressed-gas cylinders in food-service and retail settings.
penalty: OSHA serious violations up to ~$16,550 per violation (2024 maximum, annually adjusted)
recommendedAction: Secure all CO2/nitrogen cylinders upright with chains or brackets, keep valve caps on stored cylinders, and add cylinder inspection to store safety checklists.

codeStandardName: District of Columbia Sign Permit Requirement (DOB)
chineseTitle: 华盛顿特区标识许可要求（建筑局）
englishTitle: District of Columbia Exterior Sign Permit Requirement
agency: District of Columbia Department of Buildings (DOB); DDOT for signs in public space
codeCitation: DC sign permit requirement administered by the Department of Buildings (sign regulations historically in the DC Construction Codes / DCMR; standalone DCMR Title 13 consolidation has been the subject of DDOT rulemaking)
coveredEntities: 在华盛顿特区竖立、更换或安装外部商业标识的企业 / Businesses erecting, replacing, or installing exterior commercial signage in DC
keyRequirements: A sign permit must be obtained before erecting or replacing an exterior business sign; the DC Department of Buildings (DOB) permits signs on private property, while DDOT permits signs projecting into public space. Submissions typically require architectural plans with sign dimensions/elevations and the Certificate of Occupancy.
chineseSummary: 华盛顿特区要求企业在竖立或更换外部商业标识前取得标识许可。私有财产上的标识由特区建筑局（DOB）许可，伸入公共空间的标识由特区交通局（DDOT）许可。申请通常需提交标识尺寸/立面图纸及使用证明（CO）。注：将标识规定整合为独立DCMR第13编一直处于DDOT的规则制定过程中，其最终生效状态及具体生效日期未能独立确认，故此处不主张特定生效日期。
englishSummary: DC requires a sign permit before erecting or replacing an exterior business sign. The DC Department of Buildings (DOB) permits signs on private property; DDOT permits signs projecting into public space. Applications typically require architectural plans showing sign dimensions/elevations and the Certificate of Occupancy. Note: consolidation of sign rules into a standalone DCMR Title 13 has been the subject of DDOT rulemaking; its final adoption status and a specific effective date could not be independently confirmed, so no specific effective date is asserted here, but the underlying sign-permit requirement is confirmed by the DOB.
businessImpact: Installing a storefront or projecting sign in DC requires a sign permit; unpermitted signage can be ordered removed and delay store opening/branding.
inspectionCitationRecord: DC DOB issues notices of violation and removal orders for signs erected without a permit.
penalty: DC civil fines for unpermitted signs plus removal/replacement costs
recommendedAction: Obtain DC sign permits (DOB for private property, DDOT for public-space projection) before installing storefront branding at each DC location; confirm current DOB submission requirements (plans, CO) at application time.

codeStandardName: California Retail Food Code — Backflow Protection and Grease Interceptor Requirements
chineseTitle: 加州零售食品法——防回流保护与隔油池要求
englishTitle: California Retail Food Code — Backflow Protection and Grease Interceptor
agency: California Department of Public Health; enforced by local environmental health departments
codeCitation: Cal. Health & Safety Code, Div. 104, Part 7 (California Retail Food Code), Ch. 7 (e.g., § 114192, § 114193, § 114193.1)
coveredEntities: 加州境内的零售食品设施，包括咖啡馆和餐饮场所 / Retail food facilities in California, including cafés and food-service establishments
keyRequirements: Retail food facilities must protect the potable water supply with approved backflow/back-siphonage prevention devices and provide grease interceptors and a mop sink with backflow prevention, sized per the California Plumbing Code.
chineseSummary: 加州零售食品法第7章要求零售食品设施以经核准的防回流/防虹吸装置保护饮用水供应，并按加州管道规范设置隔油池及带防回流装置的拖把池。该规范由地方环境卫生部门在计划审查和经营许可时执行，适用于咖啡馆等所有零售食品设施。
englishSummary: Chapter 7 of the California Retail Food Code requires retail food facilities to protect the potable water supply with approved backflow/back-siphonage prevention devices and to provide grease interceptors and a backflow-protected mop sink, sized per the California Plumbing Code. Local environmental health departments enforce these provisions at plan review and permitting.
businessImpact: A California café build-out must include code-compliant backflow prevention and grease interception to pass plan check and obtain a health permit before opening.
inspectionCitationRecord: County environmental health inspectors cite retail food facilities for missing/failed backflow devices and inadequate grease interceptors during plan check and routine inspections.
penalty: Permit suspension/denial; local health-code fines until corrected
recommendedAction: Ensure California store plumbing plans include approved backflow prevention and properly sized grease interceptors before submitting for health-department plan check.

codeStandardName: OSHA General Industry Standards + General Duty Clause
chineseTitle: OSHA 通用行业标准与一般责任条款
englishTitle: OSHA General Industry Standards (29 CFR 1910) and General Duty Clause
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 所有私营雇主，含餐饮服务 All private-sector employers including food service
keyRequirements: 维护无公认危害的工作场所；湿滑/烫伤/机械/电气危害控制；伤害记录（300 表）；员工培训。
chineseSummary: OSHA 通用行业标准与一般责任条款要求雇主维护无公认危害的工作场所，涵盖咖啡店常见的湿滑、烫伤、机械与电气风险。
englishSummary: OSHA's general industry standards and the General Duty Clause require employers to maintain a workplace free of recognized hazards — covering slip/burn/machine/electrical risks common in cafés.
businessImpact: 影响门店安全流程、设备维护、伤害记录与员工培训。
penalty: 严重违规每项最高约 $16,550；故意/重复每项最高约 $165,514（每年调整）。
recommendedAction: 建立门店 OSHA 自查与伤害记录流程；针对烫伤/湿滑/电气危害开展培训。

codeStandardName: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom / GHS)
chineseTitle: OSHA 危害通识标准（HazCom / GHS）
englishTitle: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 使用危险化学品的雇主（门店清洁/消毒化学品）
keyRequirements: 化学品清单、安全数据表（SDS）可及、GHS 标签、员工危害通识培训。
chineseSummary: HazCom 要求对工作场所危险化学品（如清洁/消毒剂）维护 SDS、正确标签并培训员工，咖啡店清洁化学品适用。
englishSummary: HazCom requires maintaining safety data sheets, proper GHS labels, and training for workplace hazardous chemicals — applicable to café cleaning/sanitizing chemicals.
businessImpact: 影响门店化学品管理、SDS 存档与新员工培训。
penalty: 并入 OSHA 罚则（严重/故意/重复）。
recommendedAction: 建立门店化学品 SDS 库与 GHS 标签核查清单。

codeStandardName: ADA Title III — Public Accommodations
chineseTitle: ADA 第三章 —— 公共场所无障碍
englishTitle: ADA Title III — Public Accommodations (2010 ADA Standards)
agency: US DOJ / ADA
codeCitation: 42 U.S.C. §12181 et seq.; 2010 ADA Standards for Accessible Design
coveredEntities: 公共场所，含咖啡店/餐厅 Places of public accommodation incl. cafés
keyRequirements: 无障碍入口、通道、柜台高度、卫生间；移除可行的障碍；无障碍数字点单。
chineseSummary: ADA 第三章要求公共场所提供无障碍设施（入口、通道、柜台、卫生间），并日益涵盖网站/App 点单无障碍。
englishSummary: ADA Title III requires public accommodations to be accessible (entrances, paths, counters, restrooms) and increasingly covers website/app ordering accessibility.
businessImpact: 影响门店设计/改造、柜台与卫生间布局，以及 App/网站无障碍。
penalty: 民事处罚与私人诉讼（含网站无障碍诉讼）。
recommendedAction: 在门店设计与 App 中纳入 ADA 无障碍核查；评估数字无障碍合规。

codeStandardName: NYC Construction & Fire Codes — C of O and FDNY Permits
chineseTitle: NYC 建筑与消防规范 —— 使用许可证与 FDNY 许可
englishTitle: NYC Construction & Fire Codes — Certificate of Occupancy and FDNY Permits
agency: NYC DOB / FDNY
coveredEntities: 商业改造、聚集场所、抽油烟/灭火系统
keyRequirements: 合法使用许可证（C of O）、建筑许可、FDNY 聚集场所许可与灭火系统/抽油烟检验。
chineseSummary: NYC 建筑与消防规范要求门店改造取得建筑许可与使用许可证，并就聚集场所、抽油烟与灭火系统取得 FDNY 许可与检验。
englishSummary: NYC construction and fire codes require build-out permits and a certificate of occupancy, plus FDNY permits/inspections for assembly, exhaust hoods and fire-suppression systems.
businessImpact: 直接影响门店开业进度、改造合规与 FDNY 检验。
penalty: DOB/ECB 违规罚单；停工令（stop-work）。
recommendedAction: 开业前核查 C of O、建筑许可与 FDNY 许可/检验是否齐全。

codeStandardName: OSHA Emergency Action Plans
chineseTitle: OSHA 紧急行动计划
englishTitle: OSHA Emergency Action Plans
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 凡其他标准要求须有 EAP 的雇主（员工 ≤10 可口头）
keyRequirements: 书面紧急行动计划：疏散路线、警报、事故报告与责任分工；员工培训。
chineseSummary: OSHA 紧急行动计划标准要求在其他标准触发时制定书面应急计划，涵盖疏散、警报与报告程序；员工 ≤10 人可口头传达。门店需有可张贴的疏散计划并培训员工。
englishSummary: OSHA's Emergency Action Plan standard requires a written emergency plan (evacuation, alarm, reporting) where triggered by another standard; oral is allowed at ≤10 employees. Each store needs a posted evacuation plan and trained staff.
businessImpact: 门店须制定并张贴应急疏散计划并培训员工。
penalty: 严重违规每项最高约 $16,550（每年调整）。
recommendedAction: 制定门店书面应急行动计划并开展疏散培训。

codeStandardName: OSHA Portable Fire Extinguishers
chineseTitle: OSHA 便携式灭火器
englishTitle: OSHA Portable Fire Extinguishers
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 提供/依赖便携式灭火器的雇主
keyRequirements: 灭火器安装、可达、A 类行进距离 ≤75 英尺；年度维护 + 月度目视检查；员工培训。
chineseSummary: OSHA 要求灭火器正确安装、易于取用、A 类行进距离不超过 75 英尺，并进行年度维护与月度目视检查，员工须受培训。咖啡店须配置并维护 ABC/K 类灭火器。
englishSummary: OSHA requires extinguishers mounted, accessible, within 75 ft travel for Class A, with annual maintenance and monthly visual checks plus employee training. Cafés must mount and service ABC/K-class extinguishers.
businessImpact: 门店须配置/年检灭火器并培训员工使用。
penalty: 严重违规每项最高约 $16,550（每年调整）。
recommendedAction: 落实灭火器年检/月检与员工培训。

codeStandardName: OSHA Exit Routes
chineseTitle: OSHA 疏散通道
englishTitle: OSHA Exit Routes
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 所有通用行业雇主
keyRequirements: 足够数量、无障碍、标识清晰、有照明的疏散通道；门内侧可开启。
chineseSummary: OSHA 疏散通道标准要求保持足够数量、无障碍、标识清晰且有照明的出口，门可从内侧开启。后场堆物阻挡出口是常见违规。
englishSummary: OSHA's exit-route standard requires an adequate number of unobstructed, marked, lit exit routes with doors openable from the inside. Blocked back-of-house exits are a common violation.
businessImpact: 门店须保持出口畅通、标识与照明合规。
penalty: 严重违规最高约 $16,550；故意最高约 $165,514（每年调整）。
recommendedAction: 保持出口无堆物、标识清晰、应急照明可用。

codeStandardName: OSHA Walking-Working Surfaces (slips/trips/falls)
chineseTitle: OSHA 行走作业面（防滑跌倒）
englishTitle: OSHA Walking-Working Surfaces
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 所有通用行业雇主
keyRequirements: 作业面保持清洁、干燥、有序；及时纠正危害；防滑跌倒控制。
chineseSummary: OSHA 行走作业面标准要求地面清洁、干燥、有序并及时纠正危害。湿滑地面跌倒是咖啡店第一大工伤来源，需防滑垫与警示。
englishSummary: OSHA's walking-working-surfaces standard requires clean, dry, orderly surfaces and prompt hazard correction. Slips on wet café floors are the #1 injury source — mats and signage are needed.
businessImpact: 门店须落实防滑措施（防滑垫、警示牌、及时清理）。
penalty: 严重违规每项最高约 $16,550（每年调整）。
recommendedAction: 部署防滑垫、湿滑警示与即时清理流程。

codeStandardName: OSHA Electrical
chineseTitle: OSHA 电气安全
englishTitle: OSHA Electrical Safety
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 所有通用行业雇主
keyRequirements: 安全设计与布线；近水处 GFCI；不得电路过载；带电部件防护。
chineseSummary: OSHA 电气标准要求安全布线、近水处设置 GFCI、避免电路过载并防护带电部件。咖啡机/磨豆机/热水设备靠近水槽，须 GFCI 与安全接线。
englishSummary: OSHA's electrical standards require safe wiring, GFCI near water, no overloaded circuits, and guarding of live parts. Espresso/grinder/hot-water equipment near sinks needs GFCI and safe wiring.
businessImpact: 门店电气改造与设备须符合 GFCI/接线/防过载要求。
penalty: 严重违规每项最高约 $16,550（每年调整）。
recommendedAction: 确认近水设备 GFCI 保护与电路负载合规。

codeStandardName: OSHA Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
chineseTitle: OSHA 个人防护装备
englishTitle: OSHA PPE General Requirements
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 存在需 PPE 危害的雇主
keyRequirements: 危害评估；提供并要求使用适当 PPE（防割/隔热手套、防滑鞋政策等）。
chineseSummary: OSHA PPE 标准要求进行危害评估并提供适当个人防护装备。咖啡店涉及切割（防割手套）、高温（隔热手套）与清洁化学品（手套）等防护需求。
englishSummary: OSHA's PPE standard requires a hazard assessment and provision of appropriate PPE. Café hazards include cuts (cut gloves), heat at espresso/ovens (heat protection) and cleaning chemicals (gloves).
businessImpact: 门店须评估危害并提供相应 PPE。
penalty: 严重违规每项最高约 $16,550（每年调整）。
recommendedAction: 完成门店 PPE 危害评估并配发防割/隔热/化学防护装备。

codeStandardName: OSHA Injury & Illness Recordkeeping
chineseTitle: OSHA 工伤与疾病记录
englishTitle: OSHA Injury & Illness Recordkeeping
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 员工 >10 的雇主，但有限服务餐饮（NAICS 722513）部分豁免
keyRequirements: 维护 OSHA 300/301/300A 记录；但有限服务餐饮部分豁免，严重事故报告（1904.39）仍适用于所有。
chineseSummary: OSHA 记录标准要求 >10 名员工的雇主维护 300/301/300A 工伤日志，但有限服务餐饮（NAICS 722513）属部分豁免清单，无需 300 日志；然而死亡/住院/截肢等严重事故报告对所有雇主仍然适用。
englishSummary: OSHA recordkeeping requires >10-employee employers to keep 300/301/300A logs, but limited-service restaurants (NAICS 722513) are on the partial-exemption list and need not keep the 300 log; severe-incident reporting still applies to all.
businessImpact: 门店通常免于 300 日志，但仍须报告严重事故。
penalty: 报告违规最高约 $16,550（每年调整）。
recommendedAction: 确认 NAICS 豁免适用；建立严重事故 8/24 小时报告流程。

codeStandardName: OSHA Heat Injury & Illness Prevention (proposed)
chineseTitle: OSHA 高温伤害与疾病预防（拟议）
englishTitle: OSHA Heat Injury & Illness Prevention (proposed)
agency: Federal OSHA (US DOL)
coveredEntities: 若最终成文，涵盖室内外通用行业雇主
keyRequirements: 拟议高温指数 80°F 触发初始措施、90°F 触发高温措施：书面计划、监测、饮水、休息、适应、培训。
chineseSummary: OSHA 拟议的高温伤害与疾病预防规则（2024 年 NPRM，尚未成文）将在高温指数达 80°F 触发书面热计划、监测、饮水与休息，90°F 触发高温措施。若成文，门店烤箱/咖啡机附近室内区域可能触发。
englishSummary: OSHA's proposed Heat Injury & Illness Prevention rule (2024 NPRM, not finalized) would trigger a written heat plan, monitoring, water and rest at a heat index of 80°F, and high-heat measures at 90°F. If finalized, indoor café areas near ovens/espresso could trip the trigger.
businessImpact: 目前仅为关注项；若成文，门店高温区域需热计划与休息安排。
penalty: 拟议中（暂无）。
recommendedAction: 持续监控 OSHA 高温规则进展；评估门店高温区域。

codeStandardName: NFPA 96 Commercial Kitchen Exhaust & Fire Suppression
chineseTitle: 商业厨房排油烟与灭火（NFPA 96）
englishTitle: NFPA 96 Commercial Kitchen Exhaust & Fire Suppression
agency: FDNY / NYC DOB
coveredEntities: 产生含油脂蒸气的商业烹饪场所（按烹饪量定清洗频率）
keyRequirements: UL300 湿化学抽油烟罩灭火系统；按烹饪量定期清洗烟罩/风道（月度→年度）；检验。
chineseSummary: NFPA 96（经 NYC 消防/机械规范采纳）要求产生含油脂蒸气的商业烹饪场所配置 UL300 湿化学抽油烟罩灭火系统并定期清洗烟罩/风道。仅在瑞幸有热食/油脂烹饪时触发，纯咖啡/即取即走可能豁免——需核实菜单。
englishSummary: NFPA 96 (adopted via NYC code) requires UL300 wet-chemical hood suppression and periodic hood/duct cleaning for establishments with grease-producing cooking. Triggered only if Luckin does hot-food/grease cooking; pure espresso/grab-and-go may be exempt — verify the menu.
businessImpact: 若门店有热食烹饪，须配抽油烟罩灭火系统并定期清洗。
penalty: DOB/FDNY 违规 + ECB 罚款（视情节）。
recommendedAction: 核实门店烹饪方式；如涉油脂烹饪，配置并定期清洗抽油烟罩灭火系统。

codeStandardName: NYC DOB Sign Permit
chineseTitle: NYC 招牌许可证
englishTitle: NYC DOB Sign Permit
agency: NYC DOB
coveredEntities: 安装 >6 平方英尺或任何带照明招牌的企业
keyRequirements: DOB 招牌许可证（DOB NOW: Build）+ 持牌招牌安装；带照明须电气许可与年度续期。
chineseSummary: NYC 要求安装超过 6 平方英尺或任何带照明招牌须取得 DOB 招牌许可证并由持牌安装商施工；带照明招牌还需电气许可与年度续期。瑞幸带照明店招/挑出招牌须办理。
englishSummary: NYC requires a DOB sign permit (DOB NOW: Build) and a licensed sign hanger for signs over 6 sq ft or any illuminated sign, plus an electrical permit and annual renewal if illuminated. Luckin's illuminated storefront/blade signs require permits.
businessImpact: 门店店招须办理 DOB 招牌许可与持牌安装。
penalty: ECB 违规，通常每个无证招牌 $500–$2,500。
recommendedAction: 为门店店招办理 DOB 招牌许可与电气许可。

codeStandardName: NYC Backflow Prevention / Cross-Connection Control
chineseTitle: NYC 防回流装置
englishTitle: NYC Backflow Prevention Device
agency: NYC DEP
coveredEntities: 有交叉连接风险的餐饮/商业厨房（咖啡机、碳酸化设备、洗碗机）
keyRequirements: 由 PE/RA 编制并经 DEP 批准的计划；持牌水管工安装；NYS 认证人员年度测试。
chineseSummary: NYC DEP 要求有交叉连接风险的餐饮场所安装经批准的防回流装置并由 NYS 认证人员每年测试。咖啡机与碳酸化设备是典型回流源，每店须配置装置并年检。
englishSummary: NYC DEP requires food-service establishments with cross-connection hazards to install an approved backflow-prevention device and have it tested annually by a NYS-certified tester. Espresso machines and carbonators are classic backflow sources — a device and annual test per store.
businessImpact: 门店咖啡机/碳酸设备须配防回流装置并年度测试。
penalty: DEP 执法；供水违规罚款。
recommendedAction: 为门店安装防回流装置并安排 NYS 认证年度测试。

codeStandardName: NYC Accessibility (Building Code Chapter 11)
chineseTitle: NYC 无障碍设施（建筑规范第 11 章）
englishTitle: NYC Accessibility (Building Code Ch. 11)
agency: NYC DOB
coveredEntities: 公共场所的新建与改造
keyRequirements: 无障碍入口、通道、卫生间与柜台高度，符合 ICC A117.1；纳入 DOB 图纸审查/使用许可。
chineseSummary: NYC 建筑规范第 11 章要求公共场所的新建与改造满足无障碍要求（入口、通道、卫生间、柜台高度），并在 DOB 图纸审查与使用许可中落实，与 ADA 第三章在市级层面衔接。
englishSummary: NYC Building Code Chapter 11 requires new construction and alterations of public-accommodation spaces to meet accessibility (entrance, route, restroom, counter heights) per ICC A117.1, enforced through DOB plan exam / C of O and tying to ADA Title III at the city level.
businessImpact: 门店改造须满足第 11 章无障碍要求方可取得使用许可。
penalty: DOB 异议 / 不予发放使用许可。
recommendedAction: 门店设计/改造纳入第 11 章无障碍核查。

codeStandardName: NYC Place of Assembly Certificate of Operation
chineseTitle: NYC 公共集会场所证书
englishTitle: NYC Place of Assembly Certificate
agency: FDNY / NYC DOB
coveredEntities: 室内聚集 75 人以上的场所（室外 200 人）
keyRequirements: DOB 公共集会场所使用证 + 年度 FDNY PA 许可 + 检查。
chineseSummary: 当室内聚集人数达 75 人及以上时，须取得 DOB 公共集会场所使用证并办理年度 FDNY 许可与检查。多数即取即走的瑞幸门店座位 <75，可能不适用；较大堂食门店需逐店核实人数。
englishSummary: Premises where 75+ persons gather indoors require a DOB Place of Assembly Certificate of Operation plus an annual FDNY permit and inspection. Most grab-and-go Luckin cafés seat <75 (likely N/A); larger seated stores must verify occupancy per location.
businessImpact: 座位较多的门店若达 75 人须办理集会场所证书；多数小店不适用。
penalty: FDNY/DOB 违规；无证经营属严重违规。
recommendedAction: 逐店核实室内容纳人数；≥75 人的门店办理集会场所证书。
